sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Sony undergoes 1st name change in 6 decades to Sony Group
feed.summary: Sony Corp. has rebranded itself as Sony Group Corp., marking its first name change in over six decades. This change reflects the company's shift towards functioning as a conglomerate, focusing on diverse areas like financial services, gaming, and entertainment, rather than solely electronics. The move aims to maximize synergies among its various group companies and foster growth in new sectors.

feed.title: MicroVision (MVIS) Stock Forecast: Will Microsoft Buy the Company?
feed.summary: MicroVision (MVIS) stock surged over 50 percent on March 31, 2021, driven by rumors of potential acquisition interest from tech giants like Apple and Microsoft, especially after Microsoft secured a $22 billion contract with the U.S. Army for augmented reality headsets, in which MicroVision is believed to be a supplier. Despite this growth, MVIS remains a speculative investment due to its low revenue, lack of analyst coverage, and high volatility, with its CEO acknowledging the company is "up for sale."

feed.title: Deluxe Acquires Sony Electronics’ New Media Solutions Division
feed.summary: Deluxe has acquired Sony New Media Solutions, a division of Sony Electronics, to enhance its digital media supply chain capabilities for studios and streaming services. The acquisition is expected to strengthen Deluxe's technology and workflows, providing NMS customers with full access to Deluxe's integrated supply chain solutions, including localization and distribution. This deal follows Deluxe's earlier acquisition of Sundog Media Toolkit, further expanding its cloud automation resources.

feed.title: Gartner Forecasts Global Devices Installed Base to Reach 6.2 Billion Units in 2021
feed.summary: Gartner forecasts the global installed base of devices (PCs, tablets, and mobile phones) will reach 6.2 billion units in 2021, driven by permanent changes in device usage patterns due to the pandemic, including remote and hybrid work. The number of laptops and tablets is expected to increase significantly, while smartphone market confidence returns with a projected 1% increase in installed base in 2021. The article highlights the increasing complexity for IT services due to a mix of company-owned and personal devices and a rising demand for connected 4G/5G devices.

feed.title: Bilibili’s $123 million investment in TapTap plans to revolutionize game distribution in China
feed.summary: Chinese video-sharing website Bilibili has invested $123 million in TapTap, aiming to disrupt traditional game distribution channels in China. TapTap stands out by not charging a commission on in-app purchases, a direct challenge to the commission models of major app stores like Huawei, Xiaomi, Google, and Apple. This move allows Bilibili to diversify its revenue and directly compete with giants like Tencent, Huawei, and Xiaomi in the highly competitive Chinese gaming market.

feed.title: Apple adding 240MWh of Tesla battery storage at California solar PV facility
feed.summary: Apple is building a 240MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) using Tesla Megapacks at its existing 280MW California Flats solar PV plant in Monterey County. This system will support Apple's 130MW portion of the solar facility, storing excess daytime energy for later use. The initiative is part of Apple's broader commitment to power its operations with 100% renewable energy and achieve carbon neutrality by 2030, a goal also adopted by over 110 of its supply chain partners.

feed.title: Micron and Western Digital Each Exploring Ways to Buy Kioxia
feed.summary: Micron Technology and Western Digital are reportedly exploring separate deals to acquire Kioxia, a move that could significantly reshape the NAND memory market. Kioxia, majority-owned by Bain Capital, was valued at $16 billion before postponing its IPO due to market uncertainties. A successful acquisition by either company could lead to significant market consolidation, with Western Digital potentially becoming the largest flash memory maker.

feed.title: OLB Group Adds QR Code Acceptance to Its Gateway
feed.summary: OLB Group has integrated dynamic QR code acceptance into its cloud-based OmniSoft platform and SecurePay gateway to meet the demand for contactless payment options. This feature allows merchants, especially small and mobile businesses like tradesmen, to accept various digital payments including Apple Pay, Google Pay, and cryptocurrency, using just their phone. The company continues to expand its payment processing capabilities, having recently added social media app payments and cryptocurrency, with plans for PayPal wallet support.

feed.title: Judge dismisses Koss Corp. patent suit against Skullcandy. Cases vs. Apple, Bose continue.
feed.summary: A judge has dismissed Koss Corp.'s patent-infringement lawsuit against Skullcandy Inc. due to a geographic-venue issue. However, Koss Corp. is continuing its patent cases against four other companies, including Apple Inc. This development comes as Koss Corp. seeks to protect its intellectual property in the headphones market.

feed.title: Delivery service startup Enjoy to go public via SPAC in a $1.6B deal
feed.summary: Enjoy Technology Inc., a delivery service startup, is set to go public through a merger with the SPAC Marquee Raine Acquisition Corp. in a deal valued at $1.6 billion. Founded by former Apple executive Ron Johnson, Enjoy differentiates itself by having employees hand-deliver and set up electronic devices for consumers, providing an hour of installation and advice. The company has raised $230 million in venture capital and partners with notable firms like Apple, AT&T, and BT Group.

feed.title: Asian-American business leaders rally against wave of hatred: ‘We don’t deserve to live in fear in our own country’
feed.summary: Asian-American business leaders are speaking out against increased violence and animosity towards their communities during the COVID-19 pandemic. Many Asian Americans, who form a significant portion of Silicon Valley's tech industry, are using their platforms to voice their concerns. The article highlights an incident where Debby Soo, CEO of OpenTable, experienced discrimination in a grocery store, illustrating the pervasive nature of this prejudice.

feed.title: Caledonia Mining ups dividend again, says Central Shaft is fully operational
feed.summary: Caledonia Mining Corporation PLC increased its quarterly dividend for the fifth time in 18 months, raising it to 12 US cents. This decision stems from the anticipated combination of rising production and declining capital investment as their six-year investment program at Blanket mine concludes. Furthermore, the company announced that its new Central Shaft at the mine, the deepest gold mine shaft in Zimbabwe, is now fully operational, which is expected to resolve hoisting constraints and facilitate the expansion of mine capacity to target 80,000 ounces of gold production per year.

feed.title: Is This the Future of Face Masks? (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Will.i.am and Honeywell have collaborated to launch the "Xupermask," a smart face mask priced at $299. This device combines aspects of a traditional mask with noise-canceling headphones, LED lights, and Bluetooth, aiming to create a new category of wearable technology. The design, by Hollywood costume designer Jose Fernandez, is described as futuristic and will be sold through direct-to-consumer drops.

feed.title: Lovesac opening a showroom at Shops of Saddle Creek in Germantown: Here's what to expect
feed.summary: Lovesac, known for its adaptable and eco-friendly couches called "Sactionals," is opening a 1,989-square-foot showroom at the Shops of Saddle Creek South in Germantown by the end of 2021. This will be the company's first location in the Memphis area and its second in Tennessee. The showroom aims to allow shoppers to experience Lovesac’s reconfigurable furniture and highlight its commitment to reducing furniture waste through durable products.

feed.title: Meet the highest paid CEO in S&P 500, Paycom’s $211M man
feed.summary: Chad Richison, the founder and CEO of Paycom Software Inc., was awarded compensation valued at $211 million in 2020, making him the highest-paid CEO in the S&P 500 based on disclosures so far. This compensation package, centered on restricted shares, could be worth over $2 billion if Paycom's stock price significantly increases, demonstrating a performance-based alignment with shareholder interests. Richison's equity award is contingent on the company's share price more than doubling and reaching specific targets within the next decade.

feed.title: Targeting Overseas Tax Shelters (Published 2021)
feed.summary: This article discusses how Bristol Myers Squibb used offshore tax shelters in Ireland and other locations to avoid paying an estimated $1.4 billion in U.S. federal income taxes. The Biden administration plans to crack down on these practices by proposing a global minimum corporate tax rate of 21% to fund infrastructure projects. The article explores the history of such tax avoidance schemes, the motivations behind them, and the challenges the Biden administration faces in implementing its new tax policies.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon: Fintech is an 'enormous competitive' threat to banks
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has identified fintech companies, including Big Tech firms like Amazon and Apple, as a significant competitive threat to traditional banks. He notes that fintechs are rapidly developing user-friendly digital banking products across loans, payments, and investing, leading to banks playing a smaller role in the financial system. Dimon warns of emerging issues like shadow banking and cryptocurrency regulation, advocating for government oversight to create a level playing field for all financial institutions.

feed.title: JPMorgan’s Chief Sees a Boom Coming (Published 2021)
authors: Andrew Ross Sorkin Jason Karaian Sarah Kessler Michael J. de la Merced Lauren Hirsch and Ephrat Livni
feed.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon predicts a potential economic boom extending into 2023 driven by excess savings, deficit spending, infrastructure plans, and vaccine-induced euphoria, though he warns of potential inflation and aggressive interest rate hikes. He also addresses concerns about the growing influence of shadow banking and big tech in finance, China's perception of American decline, and the complexities of addressing climate change. Dimon's annual letter also touched on remote work reducing office space needs, cybersecurity spending, and specific tax loopholes he believes should be closed.

feed.title: China's Digital Yuan Is Coming. The FINX ETF Is Ready
feed.summary: China is introducing its own digital currency, the digital yuan, which could significantly impact the global financial landscape. This development is particularly relevant for fintech ETFs like the Global X FinTech ETF (FINX), as the digital yuan aims to be untethered from the U.S. dollar-dominated system. FINX provides investors with exposure to companies utilizing technological innovations to disrupt and improve financial services, and its performance has been strong, though its current China allocation is relatively small.

feed.title: Israeli digital forensics startup Cellebrite to go public via SPAC in $2.4B deal
feed.summary: Cellebrite, an Israeli digital forensics startup known for phone hacking, is set to go public through a SPAC merger with TWC Tech Holdings II Corp. The deal values the company at approximately $2.4 billion and will provide $580 million in cash, including a $300 million private investment. The combined company will be listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "CLBT."

feed.title: Paycom boss Chad Richison's net worth: Inside highest paid CEO's $211M package and philanthropy work
feed.summary: Chad Richison, Paycom's founder and CEO, became the highest-paid CEO in the S&P 500 in 2020 with a compensation package worth over $200 million. This package includes 1.61 million restricted shares tied to aggressive performance goals, potentially adding billions to his net worth if Paycom's share price significantly increases. Richison, with a net worth of around $2.9 billion, is also known for his philanthropic efforts, having signed the Giving Pledge and founded the Green Shoe Foundation.

feed.title: WSJ News Exclusive | P&G Worked With China Trade Group on Tech to Sidestep Apple Privacy Rules
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble (P&G) collaborated with a Chinese trade group on a new technology, called CAID, to gather iPhone data for targeted ads. This effort aims to bypass Apple's new iOS 14.5 privacy features, which require user permission for app tracking. The technique, using device fingerprinting, would allow advertisers to continue tracking users despite Apple's policies, which explicitly prohibit such methods.

feed.title: T-Mobile ups 5G ante with home broadband, free phone upgrades
feed.summary: T-Mobile has launched its "5G For All" campaign, which includes free 5G phone upgrades and the country's first widely available wireless broadband home internet service. The phone upgrades allow customers to trade in any working smartphone for a new Samsung A32 5G or receive discounts on an iPhone 12 with a 24-month postpaid service agreement. Additionally, T-Mobile's new Home Internet service offers speeds averaging 100 Mbps for $60/month and is expected to be available to 30 million US households, including many in rural areas, providing a crucial alternative to traditional wired internet.

feed.title: Netflix (NFLX) Gets Exclusive Deal to Stream Sony Picture Movies
feed.summary: Netflix has secured an exclusive five-year licensing deal with Sony Pictures Entertainment, granting it the right to stream Sony's theatrical releases starting in 2022. This agreement will bring major upcoming films and popular franchises like *Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse* and *Jumanji* to Netflix, along with a first-look agreement for Sony's direct-to-streaming original movies. The move aims to bolster Netflix's content library amidst increasing competition in the streaming market.

feed.title: P&G reportedly testing Chinese workaround to Apple’s privacy changes
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble is participating in a test in China of a method to collect iPhone data for ad targeting, as a potential way to circumvent Apple's new privacy features. This initiative, developed with the China Advertising Association, uses "device fingerprinting" technology (CAID) to track online consumers, which violates Apple's app rules. As a major advertiser, P&G is seeking alternative audience tracking methods to maintain ad efficiency given that many consumers are expected to opt out of device tracking.

feed.title: State Street SPDR道瓊全球龍頭ETF-DGT-ETF相關分析
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the State Street SPDR Dow Jones Global Titans ETF (DGT). It lists various ETFs that have a negative correlation with DGT, detailing their tracking indices and correlation coefficients. The data presented aims to help investors understand the related performance and risk factors associated with this global ETF.

feed.title: Tech Innovation vs. Legislative Reform: Apple’s Response to the TCJA
feed.summary: This paper examines the impact of the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) on global corporate structures, using Apple as a case study. It concludes with an exploration of Apple’s use of assets repatriated under the TCJA. The paper details how Apple and similar multinational enterprises historically structured their overseas operations to minimize tax liability and how the TCJA aimed to incentivize repatriation of these offshore profits.

feed.title: Eli Lilly and Company hires Apple's retail information technology chief
feed.summary: Eli Lilly and Company has hired Diogo Rau, Apple's former chief of information technology for online and in-store retail, as its new senior vice president and chief information and digital officer. Rau will lead Lilly's initiatives in data use, analytics, and machine learning, succeeding Aarti Shah. His previous experience at Apple included developing technologies for the Apple Online Store and retail stores.

feed.title: Eli Lilly, riding pharma's rising digital wave, drafts Apple exec to replace Shah as CDO
feed.summary: Eli Lilly has appointed Diogo Rau, a former Apple executive, as its new chief digital and information officer, replacing the retiring Aarti Shah. This move reflects a growing trend in the pharmaceutical industry to recruit consumer-sector digital leaders, particularly as the pandemic has accelerated digital adoption. The article highlights similar hires by other major pharma companies like Sanofi, GlaxoSmithKline, and Novartis, while also noting some instances where such executives have returned to the consumer digital space.

feed.title: Manager Question of the Month: How Active Fund Managers Cope With Index Concentration
feed.summary: This article discusses the challenges active fund managers face due to increasing index concentration, particularly in U.S. large-cap growth benchmarks dominated by a few technology companies. It explores how managers adapt their strategies, including considering a switch from diversified to nondiversified funds, and the trade-offs involved in maintaining performance against highly concentrated indexes while adhering to SEC diversification rules. The piece highlights various approaches taken by investment firms like T. Rowe Price and Wellington Management.

feed.title: For Two Vanguard ETFs, the Growth vs. Value Battle Roars On
feed.summary: The article highlights the ongoing "growth vs. value" debate by comparing two Vanguard ETFs: VUG (Growth) and VTV (Value). While VTV has shown stronger short-term performance with a significant year-to-date gain, VUG has historically outperformed VTV over longer periods, driven by its heavy tech holdings. The piece discusses the investment approaches and expense ratios of both funds.

feed.title: Taiwan's Pegatron to build Tesla parts plant in Texas - report
feed.summary: Taiwanese manufacturer Pegatron, a key supplier for Apple Inc., has reportedly selected a site in El Paso, Texas, to construct a plant that will produce components for Tesla Inc. The company stated that specific details regarding its overseas plant investments are still in the planning stages, though it had previously announced intentions to build a U.S. factory. Pegatron views electric vehicles as a significant growth area for its business.

feed.title: Google, Adobe and Samsung top best company culture list for 2021
feed.summary: Google, Adobe, and Samsung have topped Comparably's 2021 Best Global Company Culture list. Google led with an 85% employee approval rating, while Adobe followed closely with 96%, and Samsung was the only Asian company in the top 50. The rankings were based on anonymous employee feedback across 20 workplace culture categories, factoring in companies' handling of the pandemic and employee well-being.

feed.title: Apple and partners launch first-ever $200 million Restore Fund to accelerate natural solutions to climate change
feed.summary: Apple has launched a $200 million Restore Fund with Conservation International and Goldman Sachs, aimed at investing in forestry projects to remove carbon from the atmosphere while generating financial returns. This initiative is part of Apple's broader goal to become carbon neutral across its entire value chain by 2030, specifically targeting the remaining 25% of its emissions. The fund will prioritize projects that meet strict environmental and social standards, build on Apple's existing forestry conservation efforts, and involve partnerships in areas like Kenya and Colombia to restore degraded savannas and mangrove forests.

feed.title: Fueling the connected car economy
feed.summary: This article by J.P. Morgan explores how connected cars are transforming into mobile payment sources and data hubs, creating new business models. It highlights five key areas: digital showrooms, cars as mobile wallets, developing marketplaces, data monetization, and enabling shared mobility services. The piece emphasizes the need for companies across various industries to develop holistic strategies to adapt to this evolving connected car economy.

feed.title: The pandemic may have permanently altered Apple’s path
feed.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic significantly impacted Apple's business by reigniting interest in its less prominent product categories, such as iPads and Macs. This shift away from sole reliance on iPhone revenue is expected to continue. The company is now well-positioned to benefit from increased consumer spending as the economy recovers.

feed.title: Adobe co-founder, Los Altos resident Charles ‘Chuck’ Geschke dies
feed.summary: Charles M. “Chuck” Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Systems and Los Altos resident, has died at age 81. Geschke co-founded Adobe with John Warnock in 1982, creating PostScript which revolutionized desktop publishing, and the company later expanded significantly into various software areas. He was also notable for his kidnapping in 1992 and his extensive philanthropic work and awards recognizing his contributions to technology and education.

feed.title: An activist investor calls for a slate of new directors at apparel retailer Genesco
feed.summary: Activist investor Legion Partners is calling for a slate of seven new directors to be appointed to Genesco's eight-person board, aiming to enhance shareholder value through strategies like de-conglomeratizing and cutting overhead. This move marks Legion's second attempt at activism with Genesco, after a previous campaign in 2018 where the investor ultimately sold its position. Despite a strong plan, the author questions Legion's long-term commitment given their past divestment and the absence of a Legion principal on the proposed board slate, suggesting that a smaller board change might be more appropriate.

feed.title: AutoX Selects Arbe's 4D Imaging Radar Platform for Level 4 Autonomous Vehicles
feed.summary: Arbe announced that AutoX has selected its 4D Imaging Radar Platform for integration into 400,000 Level 4 autonomous vehicles, including RoboTaxis, over the next five years. This partnership aims to enhance safety and performance by utilizing Arbe's ultra-high resolution radar systems, which offer 100 times more detail than other market radars. The collaboration emphasizes an effort to elevate safety standards for L4 autonomous vehicles, particularly in dense urban environments like those in China.

feed.title: Michele Romanow’s Clearbanc hits unicorn status, raising US$100-million at nearly US$2-billion valuation
feed.summary: Michele Romanow's company, Clear Finance Technology Corp., has achieved unicorn status, raising US$100-million in equity at a valuation of close to US$2-billion. The company, rebranding as Clearco, provides cash advances to online sellers and has seen significant growth during the pandemic, benefiting from the surge in online orders. It plans to tap larger sources of off-balance sheet debt financing to fuel future expansion, including a new US$250-million debt facility from Credigy Ltd.

feed.title: Chuck Geschke, Father of Desktop Publishing, Dies at 81 (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Chuck Geschke, co-founder of Adobe and a pioneer in desktop publishing, has died at 81. He and John Warnock developed PostScript, a crucial technology that enabled digital documents to be sent to printers, revolutionizing how businesses and individuals could print their own materials. Their innovation at Adobe, including the PDF, significantly influenced the development of personal computing and digital document sharing.

feed.title: Logitech Combo Touch Available for New iPad Pro 12.9” (5th generation) and iPad Pro 11” (3rd generation)
feed.summary: Logitech announced the availability of its Combo Touch for the new iPad Pro 11” (3rd generation) and soon for the iPad Pro 12.9” (5th generation). This versatile case features a detachable keyboard and an integrated, large click-anywhere trackpad, connecting via the Smart Connector. It supports four use modes—typing, viewing, sketching, and reading—and offers protection for the iPad.

feed.title: Netflix’s Dominance Starts to Slow as Rivals Gain (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Netflix reported a slowdown in subscriber growth for the first quarter of 2021, adding only four million new customers compared to a forecast of six million. This modest growth, coupled with a decline in its share of global demand for original programming, indicates that competitors like Disney+, HBO Max, and Paramount+ are beginning to challenge Netflix's long-standing market dominance. The company anticipates adding even fewer subscribers in the current quarter.

feed.title: iMac features all-new design in vibrant colors, M1 chip, and 4.5K Retina display
feed.summary: Apple has introduced an all-new iMac with a compact, thin design enabled by the M1 chip, featuring vibrant colors, a 24-inch 4.5K Retina display, and the best camera, mics, and speakers ever in a Mac. It also brings Touch ID to the iMac for the first time, enhancing security and user convenience. The new iMac, powered by M1 and macOS Big Sur, promises significantly faster performance for everyday tasks and demanding workloads, building on Apple's transition to Apple silicon.

feed.title: Apple supplier Quanta says no business impact from ransomware attack
feed.summary: Apple Inc. supplier Quanta Computer Inc. announced that a recent ransomware attack would not significantly impact its business operations. This statement comes after reports that a Russian hacker group, REvil, claimed to have accessed confidential design files, possibly related to an Apple MacBook, and demanded a $50 million ransom from Quanta, also asking Apple to pay. Quanta is currently collaborating with law enforcement and data protection authorities regarding the incident.

feed.title: Apple Launches Powerful New iPad, ‘Air Tags’ to Track Lost Items
feed.summary: Apple announced new products including a powerful iPad Pro with the M1 chip, new iMac computers, and AirTags for tracking lost personal items. The new iPad Pro aims to meet increased demand for work-from-home solutions, while AirTags offer a secure way to locate misplaced objects using the "Find My" network. Apple also unveiled a new podcast subscription service.
feed.source: VOA - Voice of America English News
feed.source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News

feed.title: Is Apple a Growth Stock, a Value Stock, or Both?
feed.summary: This article discusses whether Apple (AAPL) is a growth stock, a value stock, or both, examining its classification within Morningstar's Style Box and its historical journey. It highlights how Apple's style assignment impacts investor portfolios and fund performance, especially for index funds and active managers. The authors explain Morningstar's multi-factor style model, which places Apple in the "large core" segment due to its mixed characteristics, and contrast this with other index providers.

feed.title: Charles Geschke (1939–2021), co-founder of Adobe
feed.summary: Charles Geschke, co-founder of Adobe, passed away on April 16, 2021, at the age of 81. He was instrumental in developing PostScript, which led to the first desktop publishing software and revolutionized the printing industry. Geschke, who served as Adobe's COO and president, was also known for his focus on the creativity of individuals within the company.

feed.title: The Federal Circuit Provides New Guidance for Patent Licensees Wishing to Challenge the Licensed Patent’s Validity
feed.summary: The Federal Circuit's decision in Apple Inc. v. Qualcomm Incorporated provides new guidance for patent licensees challenging patent validity, emphasizing the need to prove a specific, ascertainable injury directly impacting payment obligations. The court ruled that ongoing payment obligations under a broad licensing agreement are insufficient for standing without evidence that invalidating the challenged patent would change those obligations. Apple’s arguments for standing were rejected, clarifying that a direct contractual impact, not speculative future threats or estoppel effects, is required for Article III standing.

feed.title: Encrypted chat app Signal alleges flaws in Cellebrite equipment
feed.summary: Encrypted messaging app Signal has claimed that products sold by Israeli surveillance provider Cellebrite, used by law enforcement, contain security flaws that could be exploited. Signal's creator, Moxie Marlinspike, alleged that Cellebrite's software security is lacking and that he found snippets of Apple Inc. code within Cellebrite's software, potentially posing legal risks. These allegations come as Cellebrite prepares to go public through a merger, valuing the combined company at approximately $2.4 billion.

feed.title: Signal messaging app reveals flaws in Israeli firm Cellebrite's equipment
feed.summary: Encrypted chat app Signal has alleged that equipment sold by Israeli surveillance provider Cellebrite to law enforcement can be easily sabotaged. Signal's founder, Moxie Marlinspike, claimed to have found security flaws in Cellebrite's software after examining their equipment, making it vulnerable to specially crafted files. These allegations come as Cellebrite prepares to go public through a SPAC merger, valuing the combined company at around $2.4 billion.

feed.title: Here's how much money you'd have if you bought $1,000 worth of Netflix stock in 2011
feed.summary: This article examines the significant return on investment for Netflix stock over the past decade. A $1,000 investment in Netflix in 2011 would have grown to over $15,000 by April 2021, far outpacing the S&P 500, Google, and Apple. Despite recent subscriber growth concerns, analysts believe Netflix is well-positioned for the long term due to its first-mover advantage and extensive content investments, even amidst increasing competition from other streaming services.

feed.title: Get Growth With Large Cap Stability With The "VONG" ETF
feed.summary: The article highlights the Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth Index Fund ETF Shares (VONG) as a way for investors to achieve growth with large-cap stability. VONG tracks the Russell 1000 Growth Index, focusing on large-capitalization growth stocks in the U.S., performing well with a 60% increase in the past year, driven by major tech companies. It caters to investors seeking long-term growth opportunities and capital appreciation over dividends.

feed.title: Double down on health and fitness with Garmin’s Venu 2 Series GPS smartwatch
feed.summary: Garmin announced the Venu 2 and Venu 2S GPS smartwatches, designed to help users achieve a healthier lifestyle with new features like fitness age, advanced sleep tracking with sleep score, and numerous health monitoring tools. The Venu 2 series offers over 25 built-in sports apps, enhanced battery life, and smart features for daily convenience. Available in two sizes, these smartwatches are priced at $399.99 and aim to provide deeper insights into overall wellness.

feed.title: The Glazers
feed.summary: This article chronicles the 15-year ownership of Manchester United by the Glazer family, delving into the controversial leveraged buyout in 2005 and its impact on the club. It examines their commercial strategies, financial dealings including debt and dividends, and the persistent fan protests, while also exploring the family's approach to the business of sport, their personal involvement, and the challenges of a potential sale. The piece highlights the significant financial returns the Glazers have received and how their business model transformed Manchester United's commercial operations.

feed.title: Get Growth With Large Cap Stability With The "VONG" ETF
feed.summary: This article highlights the Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth Index Fund ETF Shares (VONG) as a means for investors to achieve growth with large-cap stability. VONG tracks the Russell 1000® Growth Index, focusing on large-capitalization growth stocks in the U.S. The ETF has shown significant growth over the past year, driven by prominent technology companies.

feed.title: The five best arguments that Apple’s App Store is a monopoly
feed.summary: During a Senate Judiciary Antitrust Subcommittee hearing, Apple and Google faced scrutiny over their app store practices, particularly Apple's App Store, which was accused of monopolistic behavior. Lawmakers and app developers like Spotify, Tile, and Match Group criticized the high commission fees and restrictive policies, such as mandating proprietary payment systems and prohibiting developers from offering discounts outside the app. Spotify's lawyer highlighted issues like the "Apple tax" and alleged retaliation from Apple, while Senator Amy Klobuchar raised concerns about "self-preferencing" by Apple Music.

feed.title: Equal-Weight Still Holds Quality for Growth Investors
feed.summary: The article discusses how equal-weighted Nasdaq-100 ETFs like QQQE and QQEW can be beneficial for growth investors, particularly those looking to mitigate concentration risk in the cap-weighted Nasdaq-100 Index. By equal weighting, these funds reduce reliance on mega-cap firms and add a value factor, offering a more balanced exposure to the underlying companies. This approach helps lessen the impact of poor performance from individual large constituents.

feed.title: Artificial Intelligence as the Backbone for the ARKQ ETF
feed.summary: The article discusses how artificial intelligence serves as a fundamental theme for disruptive technologies, specifically highlighting its importance for the ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ). It emphasizes AI's intersection with other growth themes and its role as a backbone for various products and services, particularly in edge computing. The piece also details how advancements in AI, machine learning, and supercomputing are driving demand for data center solutions and influencing new architectural developments like ARM's v9.

feed.title: Apple Investing in Hendricks County Distribution Center
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is investing $100 million to build a new distribution center in Hendricks County, Indiana, which will create nearly 500 jobs by the end of 2024. The facility will be operated by XPO Logistics Supply Chain and aims to accelerate delivery times for customers. The Indiana Economic Development Corp. has offered XPO Logistics up to $5 million in conditional tax credits for the project.

feed.title: Apple and Google Are Killing the (Ad) Cookie. Here’s Why
feed.summary: Apple and Google are implementing separate changes that will effectively end the use of third-party advertising cookies, significantly altering how marketers track online activity and target ads. Apple is requiring app developers to gain user permission for tracking across apps and websites, while Google is developing a cookie alternative that allows for targeted advertising without relying on individual web history. Both tech giants cite privacy as the driving force behind these changes, though Google also emphasizes balancing privacy with the financial viability of web publishing.

feed.title: Apple incentives are the largest in NC history. Here’s how the deal breaks down.
feed.summary: North Carolina has landed a new Apple Inc. high-tech campus in Research Triangle Park, marking the largest incentive deal in the state's history. The state and local incentives for Apple could total nearly $1 billion over four decades, primarily sourced from new Apple employees' state income tax payments which will be directed back to the company. In return, Apple promises $1 billion in investment across North Carolina and 3,000 new jobs with an average annual salary of $187,000, projecting over $1 billion in annual economic benefits for the state.

feed.title: SiriusXM acquires Roman Mars’ 99% Invisible and a bigger stake in the podcasting world
feed.summary: SiriusXM has acquired Roman Mars' podcast company 99% Invisible Inc., integrating Mars and his team under its Stitcher brand. This acquisition allows Mars to focus on content creation while benefiting from SiriusXM's resources and distribution across platforms like Stitcher, Pandora, and SiriusXM apps. The move highlights the ongoing consolidation in the podcasting industry as larger tech companies vie for top talent and content.

feed.title: What do Apple’s new privacy settings mean for you?
feed.summary: Apple is rolling out new privacy settings requiring app developers to explicitly ask users for permission to track their browsing habits across other apps and websites. This change is expected to significantly impact the mobile advertising market as many users are anticipated to decline tracking, potentially reducing ad effectiveness and revenue for businesses and publishers. The move has created a rift with companies like Facebook, which argues it will harm small businesses.

feed.title: AirTags review: The Bluetooth tracker only Apple would build
feed.summary: This article reviews Apple's new AirTag Bluetooth tracker, highlighting its seamless integration with the Apple ecosystem, ease of setup, and leveraging of the vast "Find My" network. It contrasts AirTags with competitors like Tile, noting Apple's focus on privacy and superior finding capabilities through Ultra-Wideband connectivity, despite some minor drawbacks like accessory costs and sound loudness. The review concludes that AirTags are a genuinely Apple product, both in design and functionality, designed to enhance the iPhone user experience rather than being a standalone business.

feed.title: Apple commits $430 billion in US investments over five years
feed.summary: Apple announced an accelerated commitment of over $430 billion in US investments and the creation of 20,000 new jobs across the country over the next five years. This includes building a new campus in North Carolina, expanding operations in multiple states, and significant spending on silicon engineering and 5G technology. The company also emphasized its commitment to environmental responsibility and supporting US suppliers and the iOS app economy.

feed.title: Apple opens South Lake Union towers, plans to double current workforce
feed.summary: Apple has announced plans to significantly expand its presence in Seattle by opening new towers in South Lake Union and doubling its current workforce there. This expansion is part of a larger initiative to add 20,000 jobs across the U.S. over the next five years. The company has leased all office space in the 333 Dexter campus to accommodate this growth.

feed.title: Golf balls, massage chairs, and pies: Cantaloupe lays out our cash-less future
feed.summary: Cantaloupe, formerly USA Technologies, is a payments technology company for unattended devices like vending machines, golf ball dispensers, and massage chairs. CEO Sean Feeney is leading a multi-year turnaround effort, including a rebranding and a focus on expanding cashless and touchless commerce internationally. The company develops its own point-of-sale terminals and software, expecting subscription services and more complex, data-rich transactions to drive future growth.

feed.title: Apple investing $448 million in Catawba County as part of NC expansion
feed.summary: Apple Inc. announced a $1 billion investment in North Carolina, with a significant portion, $448 million, allocated for expansion in Catawba County. While the new 3,000 jobs will be in Wake County's Research Triangle Park, the Catawba County investment will focus on real property construction and improvements at Apple's existing data center over the next decade.

feed.title: Google (GOOGL) stock hits all-time high on Q1 2021 earnings, announces $50 billion buyback
feed.summary: Alphabet (Google's parent company) reported record Q1 2021 earnings, with revenues of $55.31 billion and EPS of $26.29, significantly beating analyst estimates. Google's stock hit an all-time high of over $2400/share in after-hours trading following the announcement. The company also announced a new $50 billion share repurchase program for its Class C capital stock.

feed.title: What to know about Apple's new privacy update and why it's riling Facebook
feed.summary: Apple has rolled out a new privacy update, iOS 14.5, which requires apps to get user permission before tracking their data. This update has sparked a feud with Facebook, which argues it will disrupt online advertising and harm small businesses that rely on targeted ads. Apple, however, maintains the feature gives users more control over their personal data.

feed.title: Looking for an ETF to Hold for the Long-Term?
feed.summary: The article suggests the Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Growth ETF (SCHG) as a long-term investment option, highlighting its strong performance and holdings in major growth companies. It emphasizes the potential for significant long-term returns through consistent investing in such ETFs, despite the allure of instant gratification.

feed.title: LG Display posts strong Q1 profit on panel price boost
feed.summary: LG Display reported a strong Q1 operating profit of 523 billion won, rebounding from a loss in the previous year, driven by increased panel prices due to high demand for TVs and laptops. This exceeded analyst expectations, though a shortage of semiconductors poses a threat to future production. The company noted that IT devices and TVs were significant contributors to its revenue.

feed.title: Form 8-K IDEX CORP /DE/ For: Apr 27
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation reported strong first-quarter 2021 financial results, with record orders and sales up 10% overall and 6% organically. The company raised its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $6.05-$6.20 and announced the acquisition of AirTech Group, Inc. These results reflect improving market conditions and effective capital deployment strategies.

feed.title: Spotify forecasts weak quarter as subscriber growth falters
feed.summary: Spotify Technology SA has forecast second-quarter paid subscribers below Wall Street estimates, despite its first-quarter results beating expectations due to growth in countries like the United States and India. The company faces increasing competition from rivals such as Apple Music and Amazon Music, with Apple also launching a new paid subscription platform challenging Spotify's podcast ambitions. Spotify anticipates total premium subscribers between 162 million and 166 million for Q2, while analysts expected 166.1 million.

feed.title: Apple sales smash expectations on surging demand for devices
feed.summary: Apple Inc. reported fiscal second-quarter sales of $89.6 billion, significantly exceeding Wall Street expectations, driven by strong demand for the 5G iPhone 12 line, iPads, and Mac computers as people continued working and learning from home. The company's iPad and Mac sales nearly doubled, demonstrating its control over the supply chain and its profitability during the pandemic. Apple also increased its dividend by 7% and expanded its share buyback program by an additional $90 billion.

feed.title: These 3 ETFs Sit Atop the Invesco Fund Flows Leaderboard
feed.summary: This article highlights the top three Invesco ETFs by year-to-date fund flows as of April 2021. The ETFs include the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP), the Invesco PureBeta MSCI USA ETF (PBUS), and the Invesco Senior Loan ETF (BKLN). Each ETF offers a distinct strategy, ranging from equal-weighting and pure beta exposure to senior loan investments.

feed.title: Apple just recorded a 42.5% gross margin for the quarter, the highest in nine years
feed.summary: Apple announced a gross margin of 42.5% for the recent quarter, its highest since 2012, driven by increased prices for iPhones and a significant boost from its services business. The services division saw its gross margin climb to 70%, up from 65% a year prior, as the company extracts more profit from its hardware buyers through offerings like iTunes and the App Store. Apple also recently launched a podcast subscription service, intensifying its competition with Spotify.

feed.title: Steve Jobs Made a Brilliant Change When He Returned to Apple. It Changed the Company Forever
feed.summary: Upon his return to Apple in 1997, Steve Jobs implemented a crucial change to the company's management structure, eliminating the product-specific "business units" that had led to internal competition and nearly bankrupt the company. This shift, rooted in his experiences with NeXT, fostered a more collaborative and unified approach. Jobs's management philosophy, emphasizing unified teams rather than siloed general managers, became a cornerstone of Apple's success and continues to influence its operations today.

feed.title: Tencent Music Boasts 100 Million Listeners for ‘Long-Form Audio’
feed.summary: Tencent Music has announced that its long-form audio services, including audiobooks and comedy, now attract 100 million listeners, marking a 230% year-on-year growth. This surge positions Tencent Music as a significant player alongside Spotify and Apple in the rapidly expanding audio content market. The company has integrated non-music tabs into its streaming services and merged acquired audio platforms to enhance its long-form audio offerings.

feed.title: Highwoods Properties Inc (HIW) Q1 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Highwoods Properties Inc. (HIW) reported Q1 2021 FFO of $0.91 per share and increased its full-year FFO outlook, driven by improved leasing activity and strong performance in its Sunbelt markets. The company also announced plans to acquire a portfolio of office assets from Preferred Apartment Communities for $769 million, funded by selling $500-600 million in non-core assets. Executives highlighted rising office utilization rates, significant job growth announcements in their key markets like Raleigh and Nashville, and continued optimism for long-term growth.

feed.title: Apple v. Epic: What to expect from a trial that could change antitrust law and the mobile-app ecosystem
feed.summary: The article previews the upcoming federal court case between Apple Inc. and Epic Games Inc., scheduled to begin on May 3. This high-profile trial is expected to significantly impact antitrust law and the mobile app ecosystem, with the livelihood of app developers and the definition of antitrust law at stake. Epic Games will challenge Apple's App Store rules, arguing they unfairly leverage the tech giant's market dominance.

feed.title: Apple Finally Feels the Effects of the Global Chip Shortage
feed.summary: Apple Inc. reported strong revenue and record sales in its latest quarter, but warned that the global chip shortage is beginning to impact its business. Executives stated that supply constraints would specifically affect iPad and Mac sales, potentially reducing third-quarter revenue by $3 billion to $4 billion. This marks the first significant impact of the chip shortage on Apple's previously resilient production.

feed.title: Consumer spending, streaming propel Amazon to second straight quarter of more than $100B in revenue
feed.summary: Amazon reported quarterly sales of $108.5 billion, marking its second consecutive quarter exceeding $100 billion in revenue, driven by strong consumer spending and increased streaming by its 200 million Prime members. The company's profit more than tripled, leading to an all-time high in stock price and plans for a Prime Day sale in June. Amazon also announced pay raises for over 500,000 workers, effective next month.

feed.title: Microsoft plans to take a smaller cut from developers in its games store. (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Microsoft announced that it will decrease the revenue cut it takes from independent developers publishing PC games on its online store, starting in August. The fee will drop from 30 percent to 12 percent, allowing developers to keep 88 percent of their revenue. This move aims to make Microsoft's store more competitive with platforms like Steam and Epic Games, and comes amid increased scrutiny over app store fees faced by companies like Apple and Google.

feed.title: Car firms, tech giants feel squeeze of worsening chip shortage
feed.summary: The global chip shortage is intensifying, forcing major automakers like Honda, BMW, and Ford, as well as tech giants Apple and Samsung, to cut production and lower sales forecasts. The crisis, driven by high demand for electronics during the pandemic, is expected to continue into 2022, costing automakers billions in lost sales and risking a slowdown in the smartphone market's rebound. Companies across industries are struggling to secure critical semiconductor supplies, facing significant financial impacts.

feed.title: I'm Not Turning Off App Tracking On My iPhone, Because I’m A Total Cuck
feed.summary: The author humorously explains her decision to not disable app tracking on her iPhone, despite Apple's new privacy feature allowing users to opt out. She describes herself as a "datacuck" who enjoys personalized ads and wants to see "how far this goes" when it comes to data collection, contrasting her personal choice with the recommended smart privacy practices. The article satirically delves into the perceived benefits of targeted advertising while acknowledging the serious implications of data privacy and Facebook's track record.

feed.title: Cirrus Logic Shares Fall on Earnings Miss. Blame Apple.
feed.summary: Cirrus Logic's shares dropped significantly after the chipmaker reported disappointing March quarter results and issued June quarter guidance that fell short of Wall Street's expectations. The article suggests that Apple is to blame for these outcomes.

feed.title: Skyworks Beat Estimates. Why Its Stock Is Falling.
feed.summary: Shares of Skyworks Solutions are falling despite the company reporting better-than-expected results for the March quarter and providing strong guidance for the June quarter. Investors may have been hoping for a larger earnings beat, especially given recent positive sales reports for the Apple iPhone, Skyworks' largest customer.

feed.title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway rebounds from pandemic with $11.7 billion profit
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway announced a net profit of $11.7 billion in the first quarter of 2021, recovering from a significant loss in early 2020 due to the pandemic. The conglomerate, which owns diverse businesses, saw strong revenue growth as the economy reopened. Despite a massive cash pile of $145.4 billion, Berkshire Hathaway continued significant stock buybacks rather than pursuing large acquisitions, even as its stock performance has lagged the broader market in recent years.

feed.title: Aptorum Group (APM) Stock Live Price, Chart & News
feed.summary: This article provides a real-time overview of Aptorum Group (APM) stock performance, including its live price, historical chart, and key financial data. It details the company's profile as a pharmaceutical firm developing therapeutic and diagnostic technologies, with operations in both therapeutics and non-therapeutics segments. The report also includes recent news headlines related to Aptorum Group's stock.

feed.title: Apple’s new M1 chip creates a competitive advantage
feed.summary: Apple has introduced new iMacs and iPad Pros featuring its M1 chip, allowing the company to control its entire technology stack and gain a competitive advantage. This strategy insulates Apple from the global chip shortage affecting other manufacturers reliant on third-party semiconductors. Experts like Richard Barnett highlight that Apple's approach contrasts sharply with the challenges faced by automotive, consumer electronics, and medical device industries.

feed.title: American LGA-ORD Oasis: unremarkable in a good way
feed.summary: The author recounts his first flight after a 413-day grounding, taking an American Airlines flight from New York LaGuardia to Chicago O’Hare. He describes the experience of flying in American's dense Oasis cabin, highlighting the sufficient comfort of the Main Cabin seats, the useful in-seat power and device holders, and the ample IFE content, despite the lack of onboard food and beverage service due to COVID-19 policies. The overall experience was unremarkable in a positive sense, characterized by cleanliness, adherence to mask mandates, and an early arrival.

feed.title: Is the ‘Apple’ juice worth the squeeze?
authors: Nathan C. Goldman and Erynn Stainback
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether the tax benefits offered to Apple Inc. for establishing a new campus in North Carolina are justified by the economic returns. It presents various scenarios demonstrating that North Carolina could recoup its investment within 4 to 29 years through increased tax revenues from new jobs, local investments, other taxes, and corporate taxes. The analysis suggests that even with conservative estimates, the state and local municipalities will significantly benefit from Apple’s presence.

feed.title: 3 Clicks to $4,000 a Month in Dividend Income
feed.summary: This article proposes that Closed-End Funds (CEFs) offer a superior alternative to ETFs for generating significant retirement income, highlighting their higher dividend yields. It outlines a portfolio of three specific CEFs—Nuveen NASDAQ 100 Dynamic Overwrite Fund (QQQX), Guggenheim Strategic Opportunities Fund (GOF), and PIMCO Corporate & Income Opportunities Fund (PTY)—that collectively provide an 8.5% payout and strong diversification. The author argues that CEFs enable investors to achieve a substantial passive income stream with a much smaller initial investment compared to traditional S&P 500 dividend ETFs, ultimately leading to financial independence faster.

feed.title: General Motors’ Fairfax Plant To Remain Idle Until July
feed.summary: General Motors announced its Fairfax Assembly plant in Kansas, which produces the Chevy Malibu and Cadillac XT4, will remain shut down until the first week of July due to the ongoing semiconductor chip shortage. This decision follows similar production halts at other GM plants in Ontario, Mexico, and Missouri as the company prioritizes chip allocation for its most popular full-size trucks and SUVs. The chip shortage is expected to impact auto production significantly, with competitor Ford also cutting its production outlook and anticipating the shortage to last into early 2022.

feed.title: Epic Games CEO cites Apple’s ‘total control’ over iPhones at first day of antitrust trial
feed.summary: Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney testified on the first day of the antitrust trial against Apple, stating his deliberate violation of Apple's App Store rules to spotlight the company's "total control" over software on iPhones. The lawsuit challenges Apple's mandatory App Store distribution and in-app purchase system, which charges up to 30% commissions. Epic argues Apple's practices stifle competition, while Apple contends its rules ensure user safety and a thriving market for developers.

feed.title: Best Buy savior Hubert Joly details a new approach to leadership
feed.summary: Hubert Joly, former CEO of Best Buy, is highlighted as a business hero for his success in turning around the company by focusing on purpose over profit. His new book, "The Heart of Business," advocates for a "refoundation of business and capitalism around purpose," aligning with growing demands from employees, customers, shareholders, and society, which has been accelerated by recent crises. The article emphasizes that this new model of leadership requires an environment that unleashes "human magic" rather than top-down problem-solving.

feed.title: Apple's conflict with Facebook goes back to the days of Steve Jobs, emails show
feed.summary: Internal Apple emails from 2011 involving Steve Jobs, Scott Forstall, and Phil Schiller reveal that the conflict between Apple and Facebook over App Store rules dates back a decade. Jobs referred to Facebook as "Fecebooks" and believed it shouldn't host its own apps, contributing to the delayed release of Facebook's iPad app. This historical tension sheds light on the ongoing disputes between the two tech giants regarding App Store policies and user privacy.

feed.title: Apple announces $430B expansion
feed.summary: Apple plans to invest over $430 billion and create 20,000 new jobs in the U.S. over the next five years, including significant investments in North Carolina for a new campus and infrastructure improvements. This expansion follows robust second-quarter sales, boosted by increased demand during the pandemic for remote work and entertainment. The tech giant's projects span multiple states, involving construction, silicon engineering, 5G, and manufacturing sectors.

feed.title: XPO Logistics: Companies demand outsourcing, warehouse automation as e-commerce surges
feed.summary: XPO Logistics saw increased revenue driven by shipper demand for outsourcing and warehouse automation, accelerated by the pandemic's impact on e-commerce. The company secured $4 billion in new business, including a significant deal with Apple for a direct-to-consumer distribution center featuring advanced automation. These trends, including faster fulfillment times, are expected to continue driving demand for intelligent machines and specialized logistics.

feed.title: How wearable technology is transforming wellbeing in the workplace
feed.summary: Wearable technology, ranging from smartwatches to specialized helmets and goggles, is increasingly being adopted in workplaces to enhance employee wellbeing and performance. This article explores the growing market for wearables, strategic shifts in their application, and the challenges associated with their implementation in creating smarter workplaces. The integration of these devices aims to help employees stay healthier and more productive.

feed.title: Adobe CEO: We’re in the ‘sweet spot’ of the world’s reliance on tech
feed.summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen discusses how the company transitioned from Flash software to a successful Creative Cloud subscription service, benefiting from the global reliance on technology. He highlights Adobe's three businesses being in the "sweet spot" of technological need and his strategies for continued growth, along with addressing the future of women and people of color in the workplace post-pandemic with Catalyst's Lauren Pasquarella Daley.

feed.title: Apple awards an additional $410 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund to II-VI
feed.summary: Apple has awarded an additional $410 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund to II-VI, a leading optical technology manufacturer. This builds on an initial $390 million award in 2017 and is expected to create additional capacity and accelerate the delivery of future iPhone components, supporting over 700 jobs across various US states. II-VI manufactures VCSELs for Face ID and LiDAR Scanners, and this expanded partnership is part of Apple's broader commitment to invest $430 billion and create 20,000 new jobs in the US over the next five years.

feed.title: Lucid Motors Appoints Sherry House as Chief Financial Officer
feed.summary: Lucid Motors has appointed Sherry House as its new Chief Financial Officer, bringing over two decades of financial and technical expertise from Waymo and other companies. This key appointment, along with the addition of Margaret Burgraff, Sanjay Chandra, and Jeff Curry to the leadership team, aims to strengthen Lucid as it prepares for its public listing and the start of customer deliveries for the Lucid Air in 2021. The new hires bring diverse experience from leading companies like Apple, Intel, Jaguar, and Ferrari, enhancing Lucid's strategy to redefine luxury and performance in electric vehicles.

feed.title: The Best Tracker and App to Find Your Lost Phone
feed.summary: The article "The Best Tracker and App to Find Your Lost Phone" discusses tools for locating a misplaced or stolen phone. While the provided content is minimal, Life360 is highlighted as a family tracking app offering location sharing and safety features. This suggests it's a recommended solution for finding lost devices among family members.

feed.title: Grizzlies owner buys Taipei unit
feed.summary: Robert Pera, owner of the NBA’s Memphis Grizzlies, has reportedly purchased a luxury apartment in Taipei for NT$570 million (US$20.39 million) in an all-cash deal. The unit is located in the upscale 55Timeless Tower in Xinyi District, a building where pop singer turned livestreaming media owner Jeffrey Huang also owns a unit. Pera, a tech entrepreneur and one of the world's richest individuals, also has previous connections to Taiwanese cultural figures, having lent money for a film directed by Wei Te-sheng.

feed.title: IBM unveils 2-nanometer chip technology for faster computing
feed.summary: IBM has introduced what it claims is the world's first 2-nanometer chipmaking technology, a significant advancement promising up to 45% faster performance and 75% more power efficiency compared to current 7-nanometer chips. This breakthrough demonstrates that silicon chips still have generational improvements to offer, despite slowing progress in transistor miniaturization. While the technology will take several years to reach the market, it represents a crucial step in computing power and efficiency.

feed.title: The RPG ETF: What's 'Growthier' than Growth?
feed.summary: The Invesco S&P 500 Pure Growth ETF (RPG) remains a relevant investment even as value stocks outperform growth, thanks to low interest rates encouraging corporate spending on growth initiatives. The ETF, heavily weighted in technology, tracks companies with "deep growth" characteristics based on sales, earnings changes, and momentum. Major companies like Apple, Intel, and Walmart are increasing capital expenditures, which could further boost RPG's performance, though potential corporate tax increases might influence future spending decisions.

feed.title: Self-driving startup Aeva says its sensor can detect vehicles over 500m away
feed.summary: Self-driving sensor startup Aeva Technologies Inc. announced that its lidar sensor can detect other vehicles beyond 500 meters and pedestrians over 350 meters away. This advanced detection range is a crucial metric for autonomous vehicles, enabling them to make timely decisions at high speeds. Aeva, founded by former Apple Inc. engineers, has secured deals with automotive suppliers Denso Corp and ZF Friedrichshafen AG for mass production of its sensors.

feed.title: Fox Factory Bolsters Management to Lay the Foundation for Next Phase of Growth
feed.summary: Fox Factory Holding Corp. (NASDAQ: FOXF) announced key leadership additions to support its next phase of growth and strategic vision. Tom L. Fletcher has been promoted to President, PVG Business & Corporate Strategy, Toby D. Merchant joins as the first Chief Legal Officer & Secretary, and Jackie B. Martin has been promoted to Chief Purpose & Inclusion Officer. These appointments aim to strengthen the company's capabilities in various critical areas.

feed.title: IBM's new 2-nanometer chip will be faster than the one in Apple's iPhone 12
feed.summary: IBM has introduced what it claims is the world's first 2-nanometer chipmaking technology. This new technology promises to be up to 45% faster and 75% more power-efficient than current mainstream 7-nanometer chips. While it will take several years to reach the market, these chips are expected to outperform the 5-nanometer chips found in devices like Apple's iPhone 12.

feed.title: Apple Has Hired Cisco's Stella Low As Its Head Of PR
feed.summary: Apple has hired Stella Low, former communications chief at Cisco, as its new vice president of worldwide corporate communications. She will report directly to Apple CEO Tim Cook and fills a role that has been vacant for nearly two years. Low's appointment comes at a critical time for Apple, as the company faces high-profile legal battles, geopolitical tensions, and prepares for significant product launches.

feed.title: Sanctions-as-a-Service: US Regulators Escalate Sanctions Enforcement Priorities Into the Cloud with SAP Settlement
feed.summary: US regulators announced a global resolution with SAP SE following its voluntary disclosure of providing software and cloud-based services to users in Iran, violating export and sanctions regulations. This settlement, involving the Departments of Justice, Commerce, and Treasury, highlights the US government's increased focus on sanctions compliance in the tech sector, particularly regarding cloud-based services and the need for robust screening and access controls. SAP agreed to pay over $8 million in penalties and invested over $27 million in enhancing its compliance programs.

feed.title: Samsung to jump into laptop processor market with Exynos chip in H2
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics is set to enter the laptop processor market in the second half of this year with a premium Exynos chip, targeting the rapidly growing global laptop market. The new Exynos chip will feature a GPU developed with AMD, focusing on improved computing power and battery efficiency with 5-nanometer processing technology. Samsung's move follows other tech giants like Apple and Qualcomm, who have also developed laptop-grade chipsets, as the company seeks to compete with market leader Intel.

feed.title: Samsung to jump into laptop processor market with Exynos chip in H2
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics plans to release a laptop version of its Exynos mobile application processor in the second half of this year, entering the growing laptop market. This premium Exynos chip, developed with AMD's GPU technology, aims to offer improved computing power and battery efficiency using 5-nanometer technology. Samsung will join other tech companies like Apple and Qualcomm in developing laptop-specific chipsets, capitalizing on the rapid growth in global laptop sales.

feed.title: This KY factory makes the glass for every iPhone. Apple just gave it $45 million.
feed.summary: Apple has awarded $45 million to Corning Incorporated, which manufactures the glass for every iPhone generation in its Harrodsburg, Kentucky factory. This investment aims to expand U.S. manufacturing capacity and fund research and development for new, durable glass technologies like Ceramic Shield used in the iPhone 12. The funding comes from Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund and supports over 1,000 jobs in Kentucky, deepening the long-standing collaboration between the two companies.

feed.title: Apple awards Corning $45 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund
feed.summary: Apple has awarded Corning $45 million from its Advanced Manufacturing Fund to expand manufacturing capacity in the US and drive research and development into innovative new technologies like Ceramic Shield. This investment builds on $450 million previously provided, supporting over 1,000 jobs across Corning's US operations and fostering continued collaboration on durable glass for Apple products. The partnership has been crucial in developing materials like Ceramic Shield for the iPhone 12 lineup, and Corning is also part of Apple's Clean Energy Program.

feed.title: This Kentucky iPhone glass manufacturer just received a major investment from Apple
feed.summary: Apple is investing an additional $45 million into Corning Inc.'s glass innovation center in Harrodsburg, Kentucky, which manufactures scratch-resistant glass for its iPhones and other products. This investment comes from Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund and will be used for research and development to improve durability and product life. The Harrodsburg plant has previously received $450 million from this fund and supports hundreds of jobs.

feed.title: Harrodsburg glass plant that supplies all iPhones getting $45 million from Apple
feed.summary: Apple is investing an additional $45 million into Corning Inc.'s plant in Harrodsburg, Kentucky, which manufactures glass for iPhones and other Apple devices. This new investment from Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund aims to expand Corning's manufacturing capacity and support research and development for new technologies focused on durability and product life. The Harrodsburg plant has historically supplied scratch-resistant glass for all iPhone generations since 2007, along with glass for iPads and Apple Watches, and has previously received $450 million in Apple funding.

feed.title: China tech giants bet US$19 billion on global electric car frenzy
feed.summary: Chinese tech giants like Huawei, Xiaomi, and Baidu are pouring nearly US$19 billion into electric and self-driving vehicle ventures, aiming to capitalize on the high-tech shift in the automotive industry. This aggressive push establishes China as a major battleground for EV innovation, with companies making strategic tie-ups and unveiling their own car-making plans. Despite the hyper-competitive market and significant capital requirements, these tech companies are focused on making intelligent vehicles, with their main revenue expected to come from post-sale services and software rather than just car sales.

feed.title: Apple awards $45 million to the company that helps make tough iPhone screens
feed.summary: Apple has awarded an additional $45 million to Corning, the company responsible for manufacturing the Gorilla Glass and Ceramic Shield used in iPhones, iPads, and Apple Watches. This investment, part of Apple's Advanced Manufacturing Fund, aims to expand Corning's manufacturing and R&D in the U.S. Corning has been a long-standing partner with Apple, contributing to the durability of Apple's devices since the original iPhone.

feed.title: Apple Alocates $45 Million to iPhone’s Ceramic Screen, Gorilla Glass Supplier -- Is it a Hint for the Bendable iPhone?
feed.summary: Apple has invested an additional $45 million into Corning Inc., the supplier of Gorilla Glass and the iPhone 12's Ceramic Shield, through its Advanced Manufacturing Fund. This investment, which follows previous funding totaling $450 million, aims to expand Corning's manufacturing capacity in the US and aligns with recent rumors of a foldable iPhone potentially launching in 2023. While Corning has developed ultra-thin, bendable glass, its durability is a challenge, suggesting Apple's funding might support the development of a strong yet flexible material for future devices.

feed.title: Google Pay now lets U.S. users send money to India and Singapore
feed.summary: Google Pay has launched a new feature allowing U.S. users to send money to India and Singapore by integrating with money transfer services Wise and Western Union. This move marks Google's entry into the significant remittances market, which was valued at $508 billion for low- and middle-income countries in 2020. The company plans to expand this service to more countries in the future, despite stating no intentions of becoming a bank itself.

feed.title: Google Pay launches international money transfers with Wise and Western Union
feed.summary: Google Pay has partnered with Wise and Western Union to enable international money transfers for its U.S. users, expanding its financial services offerings. The service initially supports transfers to India and Singapore, with plans to cover 80 countries via Wise and 200 via Western Union by year-end. This move escalates competition in the digital payments sector, leveraging the boom in online payments spurred by the COVID-19 pandemic.

feed.title: Samsung and AMD will reportedly take on Apple’s M1 SoC later this year
feed.summary: Samsung is reportedly accelerating its Exynos SoC development with an AMD-powered GPU, aiming to compete with Apple's M1 chip in laptops by the second half of this year. While this partnership could boost Samsung's GPU capabilities for laptops, current Exynos smartphone chips are not expected to be dramatically changed, and Samsung still faces challenges in CPU design and the Windows-on-ARM ecosystem compared to Apple's integrated approach. The article also touches on Qualcomm's Nuvia acquisition and Google's "Whitechapel" ambitions, highlighting the broader race to develop competitive ARM-based processors.

feed.title: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's non-profit teams up with Procter & Gamble
feed.summary: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's Archewell Foundation announced a multi-year partnership with consumer goods giant Procter & Gamble. The collaboration will focus on gender equality, fostering inclusive online communities, and promoting "resilience and impact" through sports, though financial details were not disclosed. This partnership adds to the couple's various ventures in social work and media content creation since stepping away from royal duties.

feed.title: Powerley and DTE Energy Launch Industry-First Advanced Rate Management Solution
feed.summary: Powerley and DTE Energy have launched an industry-first advanced rate management solution designed to help energy customers understand and control advanced energy rates, such as time-of-use. This solution combines customer-specific energy pricing with household energy data to provide real-time rate advice and personalized coaching, enabling customers to maximize savings and helping energy companies manage peak demand and drive decarbonization. DTE Energy is implementing this technology in a new Time-of-Use pilot program through its DTE Insight app.

feed.title: Corning opens new manufacturing facility in Wuhan
feed.summary: Corning Incorporated has opened a new Gen 10.5 liquid crystal display (LCD) glass substrate manufacturing facility in Wuhan, China, co-located with a BOE Technology Group Co., Ltd. plant. This facility aims to strengthen Corning's presence in China and support the production of large-size display panels, specifically for 65- and 75-inch TVs. Despite pandemic challenges, Corning successfully ramped up production at the Wuhan facility, which is its sixth LCD glass plant on the Chinese mainland and second Gen 10.5 facility.

feed.title: Cantaloupe expands with crypto, cashless payments partnerships
feed.summary: Cantaloupe, a software and payments company for self-pay equipment, is partnering with Bakkt Holdings to accept cryptocurrencies and loyalty points at its machines, capitalizing on the shift towards cashless payments and digital assets. This move follows another partnership with Castles Technology to enhance cashless operations at unattended retail outlets. The company, which rebranded from USA Technologies, aims to drive revenue growth through these new payment features and planned international expansion.

feed.title: Under the hood of Australia’s largest sustainable ETF
feed.summary: This article examines the BetaShares Global Sustainability Leaders ETF (ASX:ETHI), Australia's largest sustainable fund, highlighting its strong performance and strict exclusion criteria while also noting the inclusion of companies accused of tax evasion. It delves into the fund's methodology, its holdings (dominated by tech and financial services), and its performance compared to other funds, discussing the complexities of defining "sustainable" in investing. The piece also raises concerns about animal testing and tax minimization among some of ETHI's holdings.

feed.title: The New Ransomware Threat: Triple Extortion
feed.summary: Ransomware attacks have significantly increased globally, with a 102% rise compared to early 2020. Industries such as healthcare and utilities are prime targets, with Asia Pacific experiencing the highest volume of attacks. Check Point Research details a new "Triple Extortion" model where attackers not only encrypt data and threaten publication but also launch DDoS attacks and contact third parties like business partners and media for increased pressure, as seen with the REvil group.

feed.title: Wahed FTSE USA Shariah ETF Trade Ideas — NASDAQ:HLAL
feed.summary: This article compiles several trade ideas for the Wahed FTSE USA Shariah ETF (HLAL) from various TradingView users. Analysis includes bearish outlooks based on a Head and Shoulders pattern and weakening uptrends, as well as bullish sentiments identified through Ichimoku cloud analysis and Fibonacci golden pockets. One investor also expresses concerns about HLAL's holdings in companies like Nike and Apple due to ethical considerations.

feed.title: JPMorgan Is America’s Largest Company In 2021, But Amazon Is Rising In The Ranks
feed.summary: Despite a pandemic, America's largest companies grew significantly in 2021, with JPMorgan Chase maintaining its position as the largest for the third consecutive year. Meanwhile, Amazon climbed into the top five, reflecting the growing importance of e-commerce, while traditional giants like AT&T and ExxonMobil saw significant drops in rankings. This year's Global 2000 ranking illustrates the resilience and shifts within the U.S. corporate landscape amidst economic volatility.

feed.title: Aeva announces customer deal; shares soar even after results disappoint
feed.summary: Aeva Technologies Inc. announced a deal to develop a sensor for an "undisclosed large company's" self-driving car program, causing its shares to surge 13% despite reporting disappointing financial results. The company's first-quarter revenue fell short of estimates, and its operating loss more than doubled. Aeva, founded by ex-Apple engineers, specializes in lidar technology for autonomous vehicles and recently went public through a reverse merger.

feed.title: GPS Watch Tracker Market Rewriting It’s Growth Cycle | Garmin, Apple, Suunto
feed.summary: A new report by HTF MI provides a comprehensive investigation of the Global GPS Watch Tracker market, including its geographical landscape, industry size, revenue estimation, and growth strategies of leading companies like Garmin, Apple, and Suunto. The study segments the market by product type (Basic GPS Watch & Smart GPS Watch) and end-users (Specialist Retailers, Factory outlets, Internet sales & Other), offering insights into market drivers, challenges, and opportunities across various regions. The report utilizes PESTLE analysis to evaluate political, economic, social, technological, legal, and environmental factors affecting the market.

feed.title: Is TiVo done with streaming dongles? Not so fast
feed.summary: Despite recent reports suggesting TiVo might be abandoning its streaming dongle business, a company spokesperson states that TiVo will continue to produce new Android TV dongles and improve the existing Stream 4K. The move comes as TiVo's parent company, Xperi, also explores embedding its software directly into smart TVs. The article highlights the potential of TiVo's streaming solution for cord-cutters and the competitive landscape of both streaming devices and smart TV platforms.

feed.title: Epic Games vs. Apple Trial, Week Two: Everything You Need to Know - WSJ
feed.summary: The second week of the Epic Games vs. Apple trial focused on expert witness testimony regarding whether smartphones are interchangeable with other video game platforms and the definition of a market in the digital age. Epic Games sued Apple for alleged antitrust violations after its game "Fortnite" was removed from the App Store, while Apple maintains its app marketplace policies are fair and that Epic breached a contract.
