Apple's stock market value hit a new record in 2020 amid these disputes with app developers.

feed.title: Stock Index ETFs Accelerate Gains to Close Out Volatile Week
feed.summary: Stocks and index ETFs rallied on Friday, driven by technology shares and economic recovery plays, despite earlier weekly losses stemming from inflation fears. While some analysts see the recent decline as a healthy correction, others remain concerned about inflation's long-term impact on monetary policy and market stability, even as a robust earnings season suggests upside potential.

feed.title: Review: Apple Inc. iOS 7
feed.summary: This article reviews Apple's iOS 7, highlighting its significant changes and user impact. While some new features like Control Center and iTunes Radio are praised, the review critically examines the controversial aesthetic overhaul of the Lock Screen and Home Screen, detailing issues with readability, icons, and animations. It also touches on improvements to Siri and third-party app compatibility, concluding that iOS 7 succeeds despite its divisive design choices.

feed.title: Review: Electronic Arts The Game of Life Classic Edition
feed.summary: This article reviews Electronic Arts' The Game of Life Classic Edition for iPhone/iPod Touch, an impressive 3-D port of the classic board game. While the game provides an engaging visual and auditory experience, limitations include a single rule set, local multiplayer only, and a repetitive soundtrack. Despite these minor drawbacks, the reviewer finds the $5 asking price justified, highlighting its educational value for understanding life's financial and social paths.

feed.title: Review: Electronic Arts Sudoku (Num-pre)
feed.summary: This article reviews Electronic Arts' Sudoku game for the iPod and later the iPhone/iPod touch. The iPod version, priced at $5, is praised for its approachable design, helpful features like Auto-Fill and Hint, and a "Newspaper Mode" for solving external puzzles. However, the iPhone version, priced at $8, is criticized for offering minimal changes and less customization compared to competitors.

feed.title: Berkshire sheds nearly all of Wells Fargo, a holding since 1989
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway has divested almost all of its Wells Fargo shares, ending a 31-year investment that was once highly successful but was tarnished by customer mistreatment scandals. The holding, which was worth $32 billion in 2018, is now down to $26.4 million. Warren Buffett cited the bank's "dumb" incentive system and slow response to problems as reasons for the divestment, though Berkshire maintains holdings in other banks like Bank of America.

feed.title: International Relocation Is Not the Only Type of Work Mobility Needed to Attract Great Talent
feed.summary: This article discusses how workplace mobility has evolved beyond traditional international relocation, driven by technology and new work models. Belgin Ertam of GE MENAT highlights that successful companies like GE are creating diverse opportunities for employees regarding their work "place" to attract and retain talent. It suggests that a broader understanding of mobility, beyond just physical relocation, is crucial in the modern workforce.

feed.title: An FAQ on the Matter protocol
feed.summary: This article provides an FAQ on the Matter protocol, which is a new smart home interoperability standard launched by major tech companies. It explains what Matter and Thread are, how they will affect existing smart home technologies like Zigbee and Z-Wave, and what the implications are for consumers, hardware manufacturers, and professional installers. The article also touches on the potential impact on open-source platforms like Home Assistant.

feed.title: A Licensor’s FRAND Commitments Do Not Limit A Licensee’s Rights
feed.summary: This article argues that FRAND commitments made by licensors do not impose obligations on potential licensees, contrary to views expressed in a previous series of articles. The author contends that FRAND commitments are unilateral decisions by SEP holders to limit their own rights in exchange for broader licensing opportunities, and that licensees retain the right to challenge the essentiality and validity of declared standard-essential patents (SEPs) without being compelled to accept a license. The article criticizes the idea that FRAND commitments create reciprocal obligations for licensees or shortcut the traditional burdens of proof within the patent system.

feed.title: Apple Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock fell 1.12% on Tuesday, closing at $124.85, amidst a broader market downturn with the NASDAQ Composite and Dow Jones Industrial Average also declining. This marked the stock's second consecutive day of losses, placing it $20.24 below its 52-week high. The article also notes Apple's performance compared to competitors like Microsoft, Alphabet, and IBM, all of which also experienced declines.

feed.title: Chips with everything: how one Taiwanese company drives the world economy
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) plays a critical role in the global economy due to its dominance in sophisticated semiconductor production, supplying major tech companies like Apple and Qualcomm. The current global chip shortage, exacerbated by the Covid-19 pandemic, highlights TSMC's pivotal position, impacting various industries, especially automotive, and fueling inflation. The article discusses strategic challenges, including Taiwan's geopolitical vulnerability and efforts by the US and China to reduce their reliance on TSMC.

feed.title: Diageo bets on data-driven partnerships with new Collaboration Center
feed.summary: Diageo has opened a 17,000 square-foot Diageo Collaboration Center in Stamford, Connecticut, aimed at fostering data-driven partnerships with customers, distributors, and agencies. The center will leverage Diageo's extensive data and analytics resources to explore marketing opportunities and address industry challenges, including the impending deprecation of third-party cookies. This initiative reflects Diageo's strategy to capitalize on recent growth in the alcohol industry while preparing for future data-centric marketing demands.

feed.title: The Dangers of Double Extortion Ransomware Attacks
feed.summary: This article explains "double extortion" ransomware attacks, a growing trend where cybercriminals not only encrypt data but also exfiltrate it, threatening to leak or sell it if the ransom isn't paid. It highlights the shift from traditional ransomware to this new tactic, making data backups less effective. The article also provides a real-world example of such an attack detected by Darktrace, emphasizing the need for proactive cybersecurity measures.

feed.title: Discovery CEO David Zaslav Steps Down From Lionsgate Board
feed.summary: David Zaslav, CEO of Discovery, has stepped down from the Lionsgate board of directors, effective immediately. This move comes the same week Discovery announced its merger with WarnerMedia, increasing the competitive overlap between the companies. Directors often resign from boards when their primary company becomes a direct competitor.

feed.title: Pega Infinity patches authentication vulnerability
feed.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical password reset vulnerability in Pega Infinity, a popular enterprise software suite. This flaw could allow attackers to bypass authentication and fully compromise a Pega instance. Pega has released patches for both cloud and on-premises deployments, urging users to update their systems to address CVE-2021-27651.

feed.title: Where and how to buy Alphabet (GOOGL) stock
feed.summary: This guide provides a comprehensive overview for newcomers interested in buying Alphabet (GOOGL) stock. It covers essential steps such as choosing a trading platform, funding an account, deciding investment amounts, and differentiating between buying shares or ETFs. The article also explains various order types and the importance of monitoring GOOGL's performance.

feed.title: Netflix in search of executive to oversee gaming expansion - source
feed.summary: Netflix is actively seeking an executive to lead its expansion into the video game industry, signaling an intensified effort to diversify beyond its core streaming business amidst growing competition. This move comes as the gaming sector has seen a surge in demand, and Netflix has previously experimented with interactive content and games based on its popular shows. The company is reportedly considering a game bundle similar to Apple Arcade, with an internal decision that these games will not include advertising.

feed.title: Nvidia sets 4-for-1 stock split, shares rise
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp announced a four-for-one stock split to make its shares more affordable for investors, causing its stock to rise by 3%. This move follows similar splits by Apple and Tesla last year, with Nvidia's split requiring stockholder approval in June and effective mid-July.

feed.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook faces sharp questioning from judge in Epic Games trial
feed.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook faced sharp questioning from Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers regarding Apple's App Store business model during the Epic Games v. Apple trial. The judge pressed Cook on the lack of competition in in-app purchases and expressed skepticism about Apple's Small Business Program. Cook defended Apple's policies by citing fierce competition in the smartphone market and the need for security and privacy for users.

feed.title: How Apple, Tim Cook give into China while pushing back against the US
feed.summary: This article criticizes Apple and CEO Tim Cook for their perceived double standard in handling user data and human rights issues. While Apple resisted data access requests from the FBI in the US, it has reportedly ceded control of customer data to the Chinese government and removed apps from its App Store in China that are critical of the regime. The author argues that corporations and free-world leaders must choose between financial gain and supporting free speech and human rights when dealing with China.

feed.title: P&G Earns Seventh Straight Gartner Supply Chain Mastery Award
feed.summary: P&G has received its seventh consecutive Gartner Supply Chain Mastery Award, an achievement that highlights its long-term excellence in supply chain management. This recognition comes despite the extreme disruptions caused by the pandemic, which tested P&G's supply chain significantly. P&G is one of only five companies, including Apple, Amazon, McDonald’s, and Unilever, to be recognized in the elite "Master" category, which acknowledges consistent top performance over many years.

feed.title: Expedia Group hires top execs from Apple and Verizon as part of leadership structure overhaul
feed.summary: Expedia Group has hired Jon Gieselman from Apple and Rathi Murthy from Verizon Media as part of a significant leadership and brand overhaul aimed at simplifying its operations and boosting revenue. These appointments come amidst organizational disarray, declining revenues due to the pandemic, and a restructuring into four key pillars: Expedia Services, Expedia Brands, Expedia Marketplace, and Expedia for Business. The changes are intended to streamline the company after years of acquisitions and regain clarity in its mission.

feed.title: Apple Card - Family
feed.summary: Apple Card Family allows users to share their Apple Card with a Co-Owner and up to four Participants, enabling shared financial management and credit building. Co-Owners manage the account together, build credit as equals, and can set spending limits for Participants, while everyone receives Daily Cash back. Participants aged 13 and over can spend, with those 18 and older also building their own credit history.

feed.title: May 2021 Product Update -What’s New
feed.summary: The May 2021 Fusion 360 product update introduces several new features and improvements across usability, sketching & modeling, electronics, generative design, and manufacturing. Key enhancements include Augmented Reality support via USDz export, a revamped Learning Panel, and expanded learning content for Fusion 360 Extensions. The update also brings significant advancements in electronics design with Stop Mask support in 3D PCB, improved display dialogs, and new library parts, alongside new generative design capabilities like Transparent View and enhanced manufacturing support for Formlabs and SLM Solutions 3D printers, plus machine simulation as a preview feature.

feed.title: Samsung, TSMC up ante in US foundry competition
feed.summary: The world's two largest foundry chipmakers, TSMC and Samsung Electronics, are engaged in fierce competition to expand their presence in the US, driven by significant investment plans. Samsung announced a $17 billion investment for a US factory, following TSMC's $12 billion Arizona plant. This rivalry, coupled with Intel's entry into the foundry business, intensifies the competition in the advanced chip manufacturing market, with both companies risking substantial losses if they fail to secure enough clients for their costly new facilities.

feed.title: Flex manufactures everything from hair dryers to the Mac Pro — what’s next?
feed.summary: This article features an interview with Flex CEO Revathi Advaithi, discussing the company's diverse manufacturing operations from consumer electronics to autonomous driving systems, and the complex interplay of politics, automation, and global supply chains. Advaithi also addresses the ongoing chip shortage, the future of manufacturing in the US, and Flex's commitment to sustainability. She highlights the company's shift towards investing in healthcare and automotive sectors due to technological changes.

feed.title: App Store Arguments
feed.summary: This article analyzes the legal, pragmatic, duopoly, moral, and anti-steering arguments surrounding Apple's App Store, particularly in light of the Epic Games v. Apple trial. The author suggests that while Apple has a strong legal standing based on Supreme Court precedents, there are valid counterarguments regarding competition, developer relationships, and the overall health of the iOS ecosystem. The piece concludes by suggesting Apple should consider conceding some ground to developers to foster a more open and innovative environment, especially given global regulatory scrutiny.

feed.title: Future Looks Bright for Cloud Technology Degree
feed.summary: Wake Tech's Cloud Infrastructure Associate in Applied Science (AAS) degree program is seeing high demand due to the increasing adoption of cloud technology by companies. The program prepares students for IT jobs in the Raleigh area, offering skills and credentials that align with industry needs, including hands-on experience in a unique Student Data Center. Graduates can expect a median salary of $85,000 and benefit from transfer partnerships for further education.

feed.title: One Illinois McDonald’s is giving away iPhones to new hires
feed.summary: One McDonald's location in Altamont, Illinois, is offering free iPhones to new hires as a recruitment incentive. The sign in the restaurant's window advertises "Now Hiring. Free iPhone." New employees will receive the Apple device after six months of employment.

feed.title: Apple Faces Criticism Over Its Cooperation With China
feed.summary: Apple is facing increasing criticism for its cooperation with Chinese laws, which involve sharing consumer data and censoring content, amid a wider technological and economic competition between the U.S. and China. The article also discusses the US Senate's "Endless Frontier Act" aimed at countering China's tech advancements, China's efforts to influence Latin American and African telecom markets, and its crackdown on cryptocurrency and its own tech giants.

feed.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum are now, decentralized apps are the future
feed.summary: Olaf Carlson-Wee, founder of Polychain Capital, believes decentralized web applications (DAOs) will be the next major trend in crypto, following the success of Bitcoin, Ethereum, and DeFi. He envisions decentralized versions of popular social media and chat apps owned by communities via crypto tokens, starting with simpler applications like photo hosting or messaging apps before moving to more complex platforms. Carlson-Wee also notes the rapid growth of decentralized exchanges (DEXes) like Uniswap, highlighting their collision course with regulators due to potential money laundering concerns.

feed.title: Stock of the week - NVIDIA (27.05.2021)
feed.summary: NVIDIA (NVDA.US) reported record first-quarter fiscal 2022 results, exceeding revenue and EPS expectations, driven by strong performance in Gaming, Data Center, and Professional Visualization platforms. The company also announced an upbeat Q2 forecast and a 4-for-1 stock split, which is seen as a bullish signal for investors. The growth is significantly boosted by the gaming sector during the pandemic and the increasing demand for its graphics cards by cryptocurrency miners.

feed.title: S&P 500 trading guide: constituents, market hours and h
feed.summary: This guide provides an in-depth look at the S&P 500, a key benchmark for U.S. stock market performance. It covers the index's constituents, listing criteria, calculation methods, factors affecting its price, and average annual returns. The article also details various ways to trade the S&P 500, including CFDs, futures, options, and ETFs, and discusses strategies for shorting the index.

feed.title: AMD's Zen 5 CPUs, APUs Will Likely Tap TSMC's 3nm Process Node
feed.summary: Rumors suggest that AMD's upcoming Zen 5 processors, expected around 2023, will utilize TSMC's 3nm manufacturing process node. These chips are anticipated to fall under the Ryzen 8000 branding, with codenames like Granite Ridge for mainstream CPUs and Strix Point for APUs. Strix Point APUs might feature a hybrid architecture combining Zen 5 and Zen 4 cores, potentially using a multi-chip module design.

feed.title: Despite What You’ve Heard, Not All Tech Products Are Manufactured Overseas
feed.summary: While many tech products like phones and computers are built overseas, a significant number of tech items and components are manufactured in the United States, particularly in Texas. Companies like Apple, Ericsson, Samsung, Texas Instruments, and Starlink have production facilities in the state, involving semiconductors, computers, consumer electronics, home audio, and electric vehicles. Despite consumer preference for American-made goods, most are unwilling to pay more than a 10% premium for them.

feed.title: Yes, You Can Invest in a Tech-Less Nasdaq-100
feed.summary: The article discusses the First Trust NASDAQ-100 Ex-Technology Sector Index Fund (QQXT), an ETF that allows investors to gain exposure to the Nasdaq-100 without the heavy technology sector allocation. This fund offers diversification away from tech and communication services while still providing growth potential and has historically outperformed the S&P 500 with less volatility. QQXT is presented as a compelling option for investors seeking growth outside of traditional tech-heavy portfolios.

feed.title: Commander One - Free File Manager Compatible With M1-Powered Macs
feed.summary: Electronic Team, Inc. has announced that its file manager, Commander One, now fully supports Apple Silicon Macs. This free, multifunctional file manager, written in Swift 2.0, offers a dual-panel interface and new features including native support for M1 chips, multipart upload for Amazon S3, and improved work with Backblaze B2 storage. A PRO Pack is also available with extended functionalities like connecting to remote servers, mounting various cloud storages, and advanced archive support.

feed.title: Samsung Display to expand small OLED line with $2.7 bn investment
feed.summary: Samsung Display plans to invest up to 3 trillion won ($2.7 billion) to build a new organic light-emitting diode (OLED) production line for small-size panels in an effort to maintain its market leadership amidst increasing competition from Chinese rivals. This expansion will boost its monthly production capacity of smaller OLED panels to 195,000 units and is part of its strategic withdrawal from the liquid crystal display (LCD) business. While Samsung's market share in mobile OLED panels is projected to decline, the company aims to capitalize on rising demand for OLED panels in smartphones and laptops, and will temporarily continue some large LCD panel production due to strong market prices.

feed.title: iPhone 13 Pro, Pro Max 120Hz OLED displays enter mass production, report says
feed.summary: Samsung and LG have started mass production of OLED panels for the upcoming Apple iPhone 13 series, with Samsung expected to be the primary supplier. Samsung will produce LTPO OLED displays for the iPhone 13 Pro and Pro Max models, enabling 120Hz refresh rates, while LG and BOE will supply 60Hz panels for the standard iPhone 13 and Mini. Apple is anticipated to launch four new iPhone models in September, continuing its strategy of offering both high-end and standard versions.

feed.title: Chip giants are making more money than ever as the semiconductor shortage rages
feed.summary: The world's 10 largest chip manufacturing companies achieved record revenues in the first quarter of 2021, totaling $22.75 billion, amidst a global semiconductor shortage. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. (TSMC) was a major contributor, accounting for 57% of global chip foundry revenues. The shortage, expected to last until 2023, is driven by high demand across various industries and production disruptions, leading to increased wafer prices and projected continued revenue growth for foundries.

feed.title: TSMC Throws The Ball In Intel’s Court By Showcasing Customized Chipmaking Technologies
feed.summary: TSMC announced new customized semiconductor nodes, N5A for automotive and N6RF for communications, at its online technology symposium. This move aims to solidify TSMC's position as a contract chip manufacturer by offering specialized processes, directly challenging Intel's plans to enter the contract manufacturing space. TSMC emphasized its commitment to not competing with its customers, differentiating itself from other potential foundries.

feed.title: How to View Your Fusion 360 Designs in AR with USDz File Format
feed.summary: Fusion 360 has introduced support for the USDz file format, enabling users to view their 3D designs in augmented reality (AR) directly from iOS mobile devices. This feature allows designers to preview designs in real-life environments, assess scale and aesthetics, and communicate concepts more effectively to clients and stakeholders. The article explains what USDz is, how to export files from Fusion 360, and highlights the benefits of using AR for design visualization.

feed.title: The Moomoo Trading App Launches New Referral Program - Moomoo Ambassador
feed.summary: Moomoo Inc., a subsidiary of Futu Holdings Limited, has launched a new customer referral program called "Moomoo Ambassador" for its moomoo stock investment platform. This program allows users to earn free stocks by referring new customers, with rewards for both the referrer and the referee, valued between $8 and $1,000. As a launch bonus, Moomoo Ambassadors can receive a guaranteed share of Apple stock for referring two qualifying new customers between June 1st and July 1st, 2021.

feed.title: LG Electronics expands overseas appliance plants as it exits phone business
feed.summary: LG Electronics is expanding its overseas production facilities in key markets like the US, Brazil, Vietnam, and Saudi Arabia after exiting its mobile phone business. The company is shifting its focus to home appliances, automotive parts, and telematics, investing significantly to ramp up capacity and improve productivity. With these strategic changes, LG is projected to surpass Whirlpool as the top appliance maker this year.

feed.title: The Moomoo Trading App Launches New Referral Program - Moomoo Ambassador
Microsoft’s Irish subsidiary posted £220bn profit in single year | Tax havens
feed.summary: Microsoft's Irish subsidiary, Microsoft Round Island One, recorded a profit of $315 billion (£222 billion) in a single year, which is nearly three-quarters of Ireland's GDP, despite having no employees other than directors. The company is resident for tax purposes in Bermuda, which has no corporation tax, allowing it to pay no tax on income. This news emerged as G7 finance ministers discussed global minimum corporate tax rates to combat multinational tax avoidance.

feed.title: Apple Prepares Office Staff for Hybrid Workweek
feed.summary: Apple is preparing its office staff for a hybrid workweek, requiring employees to return to the office three days a week starting in September. CEO Tim Cook announced that office workers are expected to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays, with the flexibility to work remotely on Wednesdays and Fridays. This move places Apple among a growing number of companies adopting a hybrid work model.

feed.title: June Deal for Futu’s Moomoo: Free Apple and NIO Share For New Users, $50 For Share Transfer-in For All Users, And Twitter Shares for Referring Your Friends!
feed.summary: Futu SG's Moomoo trading app is offering promotional deals for new and existing users in June. New users can receive free Apple and NIO shares by signing up, depositing funds, and completing trades, while existing users can earn Twitter shares through referrals and a $50 cash coupon for transferring shares. The promotion highlights commission-free trading for 180 days and free market data, aiming to attract more clients to its regulated platform.

feed.title: AT&T, Verizon Push Free iPhones for Long-Term Customers
feed.summary: U.S. cellphone carriers like AT&T and Verizon are offering significant discounts on new smartphones, including free iPhones, to encourage long-term customer commitments. This strategy provides stability for carriers as they invest heavily in 5G network upgrades and reinforces their customer base. The promotions highlight a resurgence of wireless contracts in exchange for valuable device subsidies.

feed.title: How Apple Lossless Audio Challenges Streaming Rivals
feed.summary: Apple is entering the high-fidelity audio market with lossless, high-resolution, and spatial audio for Apple Music, aiming to differentiate itself from competitors like Spotify and Amazon Prime. This move challenges streaming rivals by offering superior sound quality, as lossless audio preserves all original sound data, unlike compressed formats such as MP3s. This strategy focuses on technological advancement to improve the listening experience.

feed.title: Apple is turning privacy into a business advantage, not just a marketing slogan
feed.summary: Apple is increasingly integrating privacy measures into its products and services, differentiating itself from competitors. Recent announcements include features like email tracking prevention, IP address obfuscation, and burner emails, all under the umbrella of iCloud+. The company leverages its self-designed chips and on-device processing capabilities to enhance privacy and introduce new functionalities in areas like health and digital identity, reinforcing user trust and opening new market opportunities.

feed.title: iOS 15 brings powerful new features to stay connected, focus, explore, and more
feed.summary: Apple announced iOS 15, introducing major updates like more natural FaceTime calls with SharePlay for shared experiences, new Focus tools to reduce distractions, and enhanced on-device intelligence for features like Live Text and Spotlight. The update also brings a redesigned Safari, advanced Apple Maps, and expanded Apple Wallet capabilities for keys and state IDs. Privacy controls are strengthened, and redesigned Weather and Notes apps offer richer experiences, along with numerous other new features across the system.

feed.title: Apple advances its privacy leadership with iOS 15, iPadOS 15, macOS Monterey, and watchOS 8
feed.summary: Apple has introduced significant new privacy features across its operating systems — iOS 15, iPadOS 15, macOS Monterey, and watchOS 8 — to enhance user control over personal data. Key innovations include Mail Privacy Protection, App Privacy Report, on-device Siri processing, and iCloud+ features like Private Relay and Hide My Email. These updates aim to protect users from tracking, provide transparency into app data access, and secure online activities.

feed.title: Apple's new 'private relay' feature will not be available in China
feed.summary: Apple announced that its new "private relay" feature, designed to enhance user privacy by obscuring web browsing behavior, will not be available in China due to regulatory reasons. This decision represents another compromise by Apple in a country that is a significant market for the company, following previous adjustments like moving iCloud data keys to China. The feature, which routes web traffic through multiple servers to prevent tracking, will also be withheld in several other countries.

feed.title: Adobe: Creative Cloud app performance surges more than 80% on M1 MacBook Pro vs. Intel-based systems
feed.summary: Adobe's Creative Cloud applications demonstrate over 80% faster performance on Macs equipped with Apple's M1 processor compared to Intel-based systems, according to a study commissioned by Adobe. Native M1 versions of several Creative Cloud apps have been released, with Premiere Pro currently in public beta. Key findings include significant performance gains for Adobe Sensei features and apps like Photoshop and Lightroom Classic on the M1 chip.

feed.title: Here are Tuesday's biggest analyst calls of the day: Netflix, Apple, AMC, Dave & Buster's & more
feed.summary: This article summarizes major analyst calls made on Tuesday, June 8, 2021, featuring companies like Netflix, Apple, AMC, and Dave & Buster's. KeyBanc upgraded Generac, Morgan Stanley reiterated overweight ratings for Tesla and Apple, and Jefferies upgraded Delta and Lamb Weston to buy. Notable reiterations include Bank of America on Amazon (buy) and MKM on AMC (sell), with analysts providing brief justifications for their ratings.

feed.title: Apple in talks with Chinese suppliers for EV batteries: Report
feed.summary: Apple is in early-stage discussions with Chinese battery suppliers CATL and BYD for its planned electric vehicle. A key condition from Apple is that potential suppliers build manufacturing facilities in the United States, but CATL is reportedly hesitant due to political tensions and cost concerns. The report also indicates Apple's interest in cheaper lithium iron phosphate batteries and targets a 2024 launch for its self-driving passenger vehicle.

feed.title: Apple Music’s Zane Lowe explains how Spatial Audio will transform music
feed.summary: Zane Lowe, Apple Music's co-head of Artist Relations, discusses the transformative potential of Spatial Audio for both artists and fans. He emphasizes how this new immersive technology, with sound moving in a three-dimensional space, will allow artists to create music in entirely new ways, moving beyond traditional two-channel stereo. Lowe believes Spatial Audio will change how music feels to listeners and foresees future artists growing up creating specifically for this format.

feed.title: Premiere Pro Beta on Apple M1: The results are in
feed.summary: The Premiere Pro Beta for Apple M1 has shown significant performance improvements, with testing by Pfeiffer Report revealing it is 77 percent faster on average than comparable Intel-based systems. Key areas like importing, playback, and exports, as well as specific effects and Adobe Sensei features, exhibit substantial speed enhancements. The optimizations also contribute to improved battery life due to efficient use of the M1’s Unified Memory.

feed.title: How fast can stores fill curbside pickup orders?
feed.summary: This article examines the varying speeds at which different retailers fulfill curbside pickup orders, highlighting Dick's Sporting Goods as a leader with over 90% of orders ready within 15 minutes. It discusses how retailers are balancing customer expectations for speed and convenience with operational challenges like staffing and inventory management. The piece concludes that curbside pickup remains a significant part of omnichannel retail, with speed and accuracy being critical differentiators.

feed.title: Ferrari appoints STMicroelectronics' Benedetto Vigna as new CEO
feed.summary: Ferrari has appointed Benedetto Vigna, a technology industry veteran from STMicroelectronics, as its new CEO. This move signals Ferrari's commitment to developing high-end electric vehicles and integrating cutting-edge technology, aligning with Chairman John Elkann's vision for an electrified future. Vigna will face challenges including leading the company into electric mobility, reviving its Formula One performance, and managing brand extension while maintaining exclusivity.

feed.title: Apple's New Private Relay Feature Seen Tied to Fastly, Akamai, and Cloudflare
feed.summary: Apple's new iCloud Private Relay feature, announced at their Worldwide Developers Conference, is believed to be connected to content delivery network providers like Fastly, Akamai, and Cloudflare. This privacy feature aims to mask users' online identities when browsing websites. The article also touches upon Fastly's stock surge on the day of an outage, suggesting it might be related to Apple's announcements.

feed.title: XPO Says GXO Spinoff Should Be Completed by Q3
feed.summary: XPO Logistics announced an accelerated timeline for the spinoff of its logistics operations into GXO Logistics, now expected to be completed in the third quarter of this year. GXO Logistics anticipates strong organic revenue growth of 8% to 12% in 2022 and increased EBITDA guidance, while XPO will continue to focus on transportation services. The separation aims to create two independent public companies, each with enhanced growth prospects in their respective markets.

feed.title: Apple announces winners of the 2021 Apple Design Awards
feed.summary: Apple announced the winners of its 2021 Apple Design Awards, recognizing 12 apps and games across six new categories: Inclusivity, Delight and Fun, Interaction, Social Impact, Visuals and Graphics, and Innovation. Developers from around the world were celebrated for their outstanding achievements in design, creativity, and technical excellence, with winners receiving a physical award and hardware package. The awards highlight the essential role these apps and games play in daily life and their innovative use of Apple technologies.

feed.title: Tower Theatre - Apple Store
feed.summary: The Apple Tower Theatre in Los Angeles is a landmark of design and innovation, originally built during Hollywood's Golden Age and famed for its technology and creative flair. The theater underwent an extensive renovation, preserving its historic character while transforming it into a modern Apple Store that hosts live events, musical performances, and creative learning sessions. It offers various Apple services including Genius Bar appointments, Today at Apple sessions, and options for purchasing and financing Apple products.

feed.title: Start-up: From engineer to IoT entrepreneur
feed.summary: Teng Kong Leong transitioned from an engineer at multinational companies to an IoT entrepreneur, founding Wondernica Research PLT in 2014 and later Wondernica Technologies Sdn Bhd in 2018. The company specializes in end-to-end IoT solutions for smart cities, focusing on smart agriculture and environmental monitoring, and is expanding into cold chain logistics. Despite slow IoT adoption in Malaysia, Wondernica is gaining traction internationally and through pilot projects, aiming to provide affordable solutions by designing both hardware and software.

feed.title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:DFAC)
feed.summary: This article provides details on the Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (NYSEARCA:DFAC), an exchange-traded fund that invests in U.S. equities with a tilt towards small-cap companies. As of March 25, 2026, the ETF has a price of $39.21, assets under management of $40.41 billion, and a net expense ratio of 0.17%. The ETF's top holdings include major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

feed.title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF
feed.summary: The Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC) is an actively managed ETF with $45.16 billion in assets, an expense ratio of 0.17%, and an inception date of June 14, 2021. It focuses on U.S. total market equities with a multi-cap strategy and currently has top holdings in major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft. The ETF has shown strong performance over 1-year and 3-year periods compared to its category average and targets an ESG score of 6.35/10.

feed.title: Airbnb and Booking targets of G7 push for higher taxes for multinationals and EU focus on restraining gatekeepers
feed.summary: The article discusses how Airbnb and Booking.com are becoming targets for higher taxation and stricter regulation due to evolving international policies. The G7 summit's proposal for a 15% global minimum corporate tax and a push for multinationals to pay taxes where they sell services directly challenges current practices of these platforms, which often pay lower taxes in countries like Ireland. Additionally, the EU's Digital Services Act aims to regulate large digital platforms like Airbnb and Booking.com as "gatekeepers," potentially leading to increased complexity for hosts and greater data sharing with municipalities.

feed.title: Check out Target’s ‘before’ and ‘after’ store remodel photos
feed.summary: Target Corp. is planning to remodel approximately 150 stores this year, which will include modern fixtures, updated lighting, enhanced experiences, and health and safety upgrades. These remodels are part of a larger initiative, with over 800 locations already upgraded in the past four years, and will feature improvements to guest services, order pickup, drive-up areas, and new "shop in shop" experiences with brands like Ulta Beauty, Apple, and Disney.

feed.title: IKEA's new Sonos Symfonisk Picture Frame speaker launches for $199
feed.summary: IKEA has announced the launch of its new Sonos Symfonisk Picture Frame speaker, priced at $199, as the latest collaboration with Sonos. This flat, wall-mountable speaker is designed to offer robust audio despite its slim profile, featuring a woofer and a tweeter with a wave guide for room-filling sound. It supports Wi-Fi and AirPlay 2, can be integrated into a Sonos multiroom audio setup, and will be available starting July 15.

feed.title: Steve Wozniak Revealed as Keynote Speaker for Spiceworks Ziff Davis’ Annual IT Conference, SpiceWorld 2021
feed.summary: Apple Co-founder Steve Wozniak has been announced as the keynote speaker for SpiceWorld 2021, Spiceworks Ziff Davis' annual IT conference. The virtual event, scheduled for September 27-29, 2021, will feature Wozniak in a fireside chat discussing his journey, technological innovation, and entrepreneurial mindset. Attendees can register for free to hear Wozniak's insights into the future of technology and his role in founding Apple.

feed.title: Bernstein recommends Sea, says Singapore-based internet company is a 'super app in the making'
feed.summary: Bernstein recommends Sea Ltd., calling the Singapore-based internet company a "super app in the making" and initiating coverage with an outperform rating and a $350 price target. The investment firm believes Sea's strong execution track record ensures its dominance in various verticals, particularly highlighting the potential of its e-commerce arm, Shopee, to reach breakeven by 2025. Key drivers for the stock are expected to be Shopee's expansion in Latin America and grocery in Southeast Asia, leading to increased profitability.

feed.title: This is how Black women can be seen, heard and promoted at work
feed.summary: Susan Somersille Johnson, CMO for Prudential Financial Inc., shares her journey from a shy electrical engineer to a marketing leader, emphasizing the importance of networking, stepping out of one's comfort zone, and intentionality in career growth. She advises Black women to advocate for themselves by actively communicating their successes to leaders to ensure they are seen, heard, and promoted. Somersille Johnson also discusses the impact of the past year on her perspective as a Black woman and the importance of integrating one's life to foster diversity and inclusion in the workplace.

feed.title: Inside a factors-focused model portfolio
feed.summary: Tim Courtney, CIO of Exencial Wealth Advisors, discusses his firm's all-equity model portfolio, highlighting its focus on Dimensional Fund Advisors (DFA) funds for their factor-based weighting towards value, small companies, and profitability, despite recent underperformance. He also addresses portfolio diversification, managing overlap, and specific international fund selections like the DFA International Small Cap Value fund and American Funds EuroPacific Growth fund.

feed.title: Apple Still Has a Privacy Problem
feed.summary: Apple, despite its public stance on privacy, still faces significant challenges in protecting user data, as highlighted by recent DOJ data seizures. The company has handed over user data thousands of times to law enforcement and still does not encrypt iCloud backups, leaving sensitive information vulnerable. To truly be a champion of privacy, Apple needs to adopt practices demonstrated by smaller, privacy-focused companies that design their products to gather and retain minimal user data.

feed.title: Inland Northwest banks, credit unions keep branching out
feed.summary: Despite the rise in electronic transactions, financial institutions in the Inland Northwest continue to invest in brick-and-mortar branches. Companies like Gesa Credit Union, Numerica Credit Union, and Banner Bank are expanding or renovating physical locations, recognizing that while digital banking is prevalent, clients still value face-to-face services and physical accessibility for relationship building and complex financial discussions. This trend suggests a hybrid approach where physical branches evolve to complement digital offerings, focusing on customer experience and trust.

feed.title: Kopin Pancake Optics to ‘Reshape’ MR Headsets
feed.summary: Kopin Corp has developed fully-plastic Pancake optics designed to significantly improve virtual, augmented, and mixed reality headsets. These new optics offer magnified image quality with a thinner and lighter form factor, aiming to overcome the bulkiness that has hindered consumer adoption. Kopin has filed multiple patents for this technology, which promises to make MR headsets much lighter and more compact.

feed.title: Yu v. Apple Settles It: The CAFC is Suffering from a Prolonged Version of Alice in Wonderland Syndrome
feed.summary: This article criticizes the Federal Circuit's handling of patent eligibility cases, particularly Yu v. Apple, arguing that the court is suffering from a "prolonged version of Alice in Wonderland Syndrome." The author contends that the court's interpretations of patent eligibility under 35 U.S.C. 101 are flawed, leading to tangible inventions being deemed abstract, and that the court disregards proper claim construction and statutory guidelines. The piece calls for Congress to address the perceived issues with patent eligibility jurisprudence.

feed.title: Will Intuitive Surgical Be a Trillion-Dollar Stock by 2031?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) can achieve a trillion-dollar market capitalization by 2031. It compares Intuitive's current market cap and growth trajectory to trillion-dollar tech companies, noting its dominance in robotic surgery market but also potential challenges from rivals and revenue growth limitations. Although reaching $1 trillion is deemed unlikely, the author remains optimistic about Intuitive Surgical as a strong long-term investment for healthcare portfolios.

feed.title: Wang: $ZH Remains My Top Pick
feed.summary: Felix Wang, a China analyst at HedgeyeTech, reiterates Zhihu (ZH) as his top pick, highlighting its significant outperformance with a 40% gain since being designated a "best Long" three months prior. The article explains that ZH is focused on expanding its content e-commerce solutions (Zhi+) and establishing itself as a new content leader, particularly by embracing "videolization" and attracting diverse content creators. Wang draws a parallel between ZH and Bilibili (BILI), suggesting ZH has substantial growth potential if it can emulate BILI's success to reach a similar market capitalization.

feed.title: William Kaufman, Travelers Cos. Seal $185M Loan for 437 Madison Avenue
feed.summary: The William Kaufman Organization and The Travelers Companies have closed a $185 million refinance loan for 437 Madison Avenue, with Wells Fargo providing the new debt. This loan retires $145 million in existing debt from Bank of America and Apple Bank. Eastdil Secured brokered the deal on behalf of the owners.

feed.title: App Tracking Transparency and the Future of App Measurement
feed.summary: This whitepaper from Comscore discusses the implications of App Tracking Transparency (ATT) and other privacy changes, including iOS 14.5, on mobile app measurement for content publishers and advertisers. It explores how Comscore and similar providers can address measurement challenges while upholding user privacy. The paper highlights the evolving landscape of web browser environments and privacy regulations.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway: Interesting Facts About The Company
feed.summary: This article delves into interesting facts about Berkshire Hathaway, led by Warren Buffett, based on insights from its 2020 annual report. It highlights Berkshire's unique approach as a conglomerate, its diverse portfolio including 100% and less-than-100% owned companies, and its strategy of share buybacks to enhance shareholder value. The piece also discusses Buffett's candid acknowledgment of a past investment mistake, the company's two classes of shares (BRK.A and BRK.B), its no-dividend policy, and the succession plan for its leadership.

feed.title: Looking to Grow Your Dividends? Tap Into 'TDVG'
feed.summary: As inflation rises and interest rates remain low, dividend growth strategies are gaining importance for investors. The T. Rowe Price Dividend Growth ETF (TDVG) offers an actively managed approach to capitalize on this trend by investing in companies with strong dividend growth potential. TDVG provides flexibility with its unconstrained index approach, allowing it to seek undervalued stocks, and boasts holdings in major companies like Microsoft, Apple, and Visa.

feed.title: Amazon and Apple: A tale of two closed-loop visions
feed.summary: This article contrasts the circular economy visions of Amazon and Apple, detailing their different approaches to sustainability despite their massive scale and technological leadership. It highlights Amazon's focus on customer education and packaging reduction, and Apple's emphasis on supply chain engagement, material recycling, and product design for circularity. Both companies integrate social justice into their sustainability efforts, supporting initiatives for underserved communities.

feed.title: What to expect from Microsoft's next version of Windows
feed.summary: Microsoft is preparing to unveil a significant update to Windows, which a leaked build suggests will be called Windows 11. This new version is expected to feature design changes, including a centered taskbar and rounded window corners, along with modern features like enhanced window snapping and better multi-monitor support. Analysts are also watching for potential business model shifts, such as a "Windows-as-a-Service" offering or stronger M365 integration.

feed.title: Why Steven Spielberg’s Netflix deal is not as groundbreaking as Netflix wants you to think
feed.summary: Steven Spielberg's production company, Amblin Partners, struck a deal with Netflix, leading to significant media attention. While Netflix aims to leverage Spielberg's name for content and subscribers, the article argues the deal is not as groundbreaking as it seems, as Amblin's existing theatrical commitments with Universal remain unaffected, and Spielberg's personal directing projects are not exclusive to Netflix. The financial details and number of films in the Netflix deal also remain unspecified, dampening its perceived impact.

feed.title: S&P ETF Fortunes Reverse In 2021
feed.summary: In 2021, S&P 500 ETFs with alternative weighting methodologies, such as reverse market cap (YPS) and equal weight (RSP), began outperforming the traditional market-cap-weighted SPDR S&P 500 Trust (SPY). This shift is attributed to the underperformance of large-cap tech stocks and the better performance of mid-cap and smaller companies benefiting from the U.S. economic reopening. While alternative strategies show short-term gains, SPY maintains long-term outperformance and significant cost advantages due to its size and liquidity.

feed.title: Microsoft challenges Apple's business model with new Windows 11 operating system
feed.summary: Microsoft unveiled Windows 11, its first major operating system update since 2015, which directly challenges Apple's lucrative App Store model. The new Windows Store will allow developers to use their own in-app payment systems without commissions and enable Android apps to run on Windows PCs. This move contrasts sharply with Apple's "walled garden" approach and also competes with Google Play Store's commission structure, signaling a significant shift in Microsoft's strategy to attract developers and consumers.

feed.title: Passbook by Remitly Launches Suite of New Features
feed.summary: Remitly's Passbook app has launched several new digital banking features to better serve immigrants, including a streamlined identity verification process, faster bank transfers, early payday access, person-to-person payments, and immediate activation of the Passbook Visa debit card. These enhancements aim to address common barriers immigrants face in accessing banking services, such as high fees and restrictive documentation. The company emphasizes its commitment to financial inclusion and responding to direct customer feedback to transform traditional financial processes.

feed.title: Google delays Chrome's blocking of tracking cookies to late 2023
feed.summary: Google has announced a delay in fully blocking tracking cookies in its Chrome web browser until late 2023, nearly two years later than initially planned. This decision comes amidst antitrust concerns from competitors and regulators, with Google citing the need for sufficient time for public discussion and for the advertising industry to adapt. The delay has positively impacted shares of companies reliant on cookies, while regulatory bodies like Britain's CMA and the EU's competition commission are actively involved in overseeing Google's changes.

feed.title: Apple Explores Bigger iPads and Reshuffles Its Car Team
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly exploring the development of larger iPad models and making significant changes to its secretive Apple Car project. Additionally, the company Peloton is positioning itself to compete with the Apple Watch in the wearable technology market, while Amazon is delving into augmented reality.
